WorkBridge PH

A Web-Based Unified Employment Platform for Decent Work Access

Major Course Output · IT305 Web Systems and Technologies
Northwest Samar State University · Calbayog City, Samar Authors Jhon Clarence Rulona
Jovannie Mataganas
John Lienard Safuentes
Cyril James Garnica
Jahiel Tapiador Submitted for Intellectual Property Protection May 11, 2026

.gitignore · 83 lines
   1# =====================================================
   2# WorkBridge PH — repository ignore rules
   3# =====================================================
   4
   5# ---- Secrets — NEVER commit these ----
   6# .env contains APP_KEY (decrypts sessions), DB password, mail password.
   7# Leak-prevention is the #1 reason this file exists.
   8backend/.env
   9backend/.env.backup
  10backend/.env.production
  11backend/.env.*.local
  12.env
  13.env.*
  14!**/.env.example
  15!**/.env.production.example
  16
  17# ---- Auth tokens / generated keys ----
  18backend/auth.json
  19backend/storage/*.key
  20backend/storage/oauth-private.key
  21backend/storage/oauth-public.key
  22
  23# ---- Dependencies ----
  24node_modules/
  25backend/vendor/
  26
  27# ---- Build artifacts ----
  28backend/public/build/
  29backend/public/hot/
  30backend/public/storage    # symlink — regenerated via `php artisan storage:link`
  31
  32# ---- User-uploaded content (regenerates from seeder) ----
  33backend/storage/app/private/
  34backend/storage/app/public/avatars/
  35backend/storage/app/resumes/
  36
  37# ---- Laravel runtime ----
  38backend/storage/framework/cache/
  39backend/storage/framework/sessions/
  40backend/storage/framework/testing/
  41backend/storage/framework/views/
  42backend/storage/logs/
  43backend/bootstrap/cache/
  44
  45# ---- Test artifacts ----
  46test-results/
  47playwright-report/
  48blob-report/
  49.phpunit.cache/
  50backend/.phpunit.cache/
  51backend/.phpunit.result.cache
  52
  53# ---- IDE / editor state ----
  54.idea/
  55.vscode/
  56.fleet/
  57.nova/
  58.zed/
  59.cursor/
  60.phpactor.json
  61backend/.phpactor.json
  62*.iml
  63*.swp
  64*.swo
  65
  66# ---- OS noise ----
  67.DS_Store
  68Thumbs.db
  69desktop.ini
  70ehthumbs.db
  71$RECYCLE.BIN/
  72
  73# ---- Logs ----
  74*.log
  75npm-debug.log*
  76yarn-debug.log*
  77yarn-error.log*
  78
  79# ---- Misc ----
  80*.tar.gz
  81*.zip
  82coverage/
  83
Documentation/POSTER_CONTENT.md · 240 lines
   1# WorkBridge PH — Poster Content Pack
   2
   3**Format:** 3 ft × 6 ft (36" × 72") — portrait orientation recommended
   4**Print resolution:** 150 DPI minimum (5400 × 10800 px at 150 DPI)
   5**Design tool:** Canva (search "Research Poster" templates) or PowerPoint (Custom Slide Size 36"×72")
   6
   7The text below is **paste-ready**. Copy each block into your chosen template.
   8
   9---
  10
  11## 🟪 HEADER BLOCK *(top of poster, ~10% height)*
  12
  13### Big title (use 200pt+ font)
  14```
  15WorkBridge PH
  16```
  17
  18### Subtitle (use 60pt font)
  19```
  20A Web-Based Unified Employment Platform for Decent Work Access
  21```
  22
  23### Authoring line (use 32pt, italic)
  24```
  25Major Course Output · IT305 Web Systems and Technologies
  26Northwest Samar State University · Calbayog City, Samar
  27```
  28
  29### Group members line (use 28pt)
  30```
  31Group Members:
  32Jhon Clarence Rulona · Jovannie Mataganas · John Lienard Safuentes
  33Cyril James Garnica · Jahiel Tapiador
  34```
  35
  36### Submission line (use 24pt, slightly smaller than members)
  37```
  38Submitted May 15, 2026
  39```
  40
  41---
  42
  43## 🟥 PROBLEMS *(left column or middle-left)*
  44
  45### Section title (40pt, bold)
  46```
  47The Problem
  48```
  49
  50### Body (use 22–24pt for poster legibility from 1 meter)
  51```
  52The Philippines faces persistent employment friction. Job seekers
  53struggle to find legitimate, fairly-paid work. Employers cannot
  54efficiently reach qualified talent. Existing platforms cater
  55overwhelmingly to white-collar roles, leaving blue-collar and
  56household workers underserved. Online job scams target the most
  57vulnerable.
  58
  59These barriers obstruct the United Nations Sustainable Development
  60Goals — most directly SDG 8 (Decent Work and Economic Growth),
  61SDG 1 (No Poverty), and SDG 10 (Reduced Inequalities).
  62```
  63
  64### Bullet list (after the paragraph, use 24pt with icons if your template supports)
  65```
  66• Mismatch between seekers and employers
  67• No unified platform for blue-collar / household workers
  68• Scam listings exploit job hunters
  69• Manual hiring workflows waste time on both sides
  70• Lack of transparent application tracking
  71```
  72
  73---
  74
  75## 🟩 SOLUTION *(middle column or center)*
  76
  77### Section title (40pt, bold)
  78```
  79Our Solution
  80```
  81
  82### Body (24pt)
  83```
  84WorkBridge PH is a single platform unifying three audiences —
  85job seekers, employers, and platform administrators — across
  86white-collar, blue-collar, and service-sector employment.
  87
  88Every employer is verified before posting jobs. Every administrative
  89action is audit-logged. Every applicant has skill-match scoring,
  90status tracking, and direct messaging with hiring managers.
  91```
  92
  93### Feature list (24pt with checkmarks)
  94```
  95✓ Three role-based dashboards with charts and real-time analytics
  96✓ Verified employer system — admins approve before listings go live
  97✓ Skill-based match scoring on every application
  98✓ Application timeline — seekers track progress in real time
  99✓ Resume upload + private storage with authenticated download
 100✓ Cross-role messaging restricted to seeker ↔ employer pairs
 101✓ Mobile-responsive — works on phones, tablets, and desktops
 102✓ Full dark mode for accessibility and battery savings
 103✓ Audit log — every admin decision is traceable
 104```
 105
 106---
 107
 108## 🟦 PROGRAMMING & SCRIPTING LANGUAGES *(right column)*
 109
 110### Section title (40pt, bold)
 111```
 112Technology Stack
 113```
 114
 115### Three sub-sections (28pt headings, 22pt body)
 116
 117**Backend**
 118```
 119PHP 8.2 with Laravel 11 framework
 120MySQL 8.0 relational database
 121Laravel Sanctum for API authentication
 122Eloquent ORM with 17 normalized tables
 123PHPUnit for automated testing
 124```
 125
 126**Frontend**
 127```
 128HTML5 + CSS3 with custom design tokens
 129Vanilla JavaScript (ES Modules)
 130Bootstrap 5 for responsive layout
 131Chart.js for analytics visualizations
 132Bootstrap Icons for the icon set
 133```
 134
 135**Tooling and Quality**
 136```
 137Playwright for end-to-end browser testing
 138Git + GitHub for version control
 139XAMPP for local development on Windows
 14014 automated tests (7 backend + 7 frontend)
 141```
 142
 143---
 144
 145## 🟨 SECURITY & ARCHITECTURE *(bottom band — small but important for technical credibility)*
 146
 147### Section title (32pt)
 148```
 149Security and Architecture Highlights
 150```
 151
 152### Two-column list (20pt)
 153```
 154• Bearer token authentication (revoked on password reset)
 155• Role-based access control (seeker / employer / admin)
 156• Rate limiting on login + register + uploads
 157• XSS protection via output escaping on all dynamic content
 158• IDOR prevention through inline ownership checks
 159• Private file storage for resumes (UUID filenames)
 160• CORS hardening with explicit origin allowlist
 161• 14 automated regression tests prevent class-of-bug recurrence
 162```
 163
 164---
 165
 166## 🟧 FOOTER BLOCK *(very bottom)*
 167
 168### QR + repository (place QR code on the right, text on the left)
 169```
 170View source code:
 171github.com/SEPrimo19/workbridge-ph
 172```
 173
 174*(Generate the QR at qrcode-monkey.com or use Canva's built-in QR widget — point it to the GitHub URL.)*
 175
 176### Course instructor acknowledgment line
 177```
 178Submitted to [Instructor Name] — IT305 Web Systems and Technologies
 179Northwest Samar State University · College of Computing Studies · 2026
 180```
 181
 182---
 183
 184## Visual design guidance
 185
 186### Color palette (matches the app's actual UI)
 187- **Primary indigo:** `#4F46E5`
 188- **Verified teal:** `#0D9488`
 189- **Warning amber:** `#F59E0B`
 190- **Rose accent:** `#E11D48`
 191- **Neutral slate:** `#64748B`
 192- **Light background:** `#F7F8FA`
 193- **Dark text:** `#111827`
 194
 195### Typography
 196- **Headings:** A bold sans-serif (Inter, Poppins, Montserrat)
 197- **Body:** Same family in regular weight, never below 22pt for poster legibility
 198- **Code / tech-stack lines:** Monospace (Fira Code, JetBrains Mono, Courier)
 199
 200### Layout sketch (portrait 3'×6')
 201
 202```
 203┌─────────────────────────────┐
 204│         HEADER              │ ← System name + subtitle + group
 205├──────────┬──────────────────┤
 206│          │                  │
 207│ PROBLEM  │   SCREENSHOT     │ ← actual UI screenshot for visual punch
 208│          │                  │   (login, dashboard, job-detail collage)
 209│          │                  │
 210├──────────┼──────────────────┤
 211│          │                  │
 212│ SOLUTION │   TECH STACK     │
 213│          │                  │
 214│          │                  │
 215├──────────┴──────────────────┤
 216│      SECURITY band          │
 217├─────────────────────────────┤
 218│  QR  · GitHub URL · Footer  │
 219└─────────────────────────────┘
 220```
 221
 222### What to take screenshots of (place center-right column)
 2231. The seeker dashboard with charts
 2242. The job-detail page (showing avatar + verified badge + apply card)
 2253. The admin analytics page with all 4 charts visible
 2264. Optional: a phone mockup of the mobile drawer open
 227
 228Use **`Win+Shift+S`** (Snipping Tool) to capture, then drop into your poster template.
 229
 230---
 231
 232## Print services in Calbayog / Samar (rough cost estimates)
 233
 234- 3'×6' tarpaulin print: **₱400–₱800** depending on shop
 235- Allow **48-hour turnaround** at most local printers
 236- Bring a USB drive with the final PDF to the print shop
 237- Common shops: print services near NwSSU campus, public market printers
 238
 239**Recommendation:** finalize design by **May 12** so you have May 13–14 buffer for printer delays.
 240
Documentation/VIDEO_DEMO_SCRIPT.md · 189 lines
   1# WorkBridge PH — Video Demo Script
   2
   3**Target runtime:** 4–5 minutes
   4**Recording tool:** OBS Studio (free) or Windows Game Bar (`Win+G`) or Loom
   5**Resolution:** 1080p, 1920×1080 viewport
   6**Audio:** Speak clearly, slightly slower than natural pace (the instructor may watch on phone speakers, not headphones)
   7
   8---
   9
  10## 👥 Presenter assignments
  11
  12Each member narrates one segment. Swap roles if anyone has voice/recording issues — these are defaults.
  13
  14| Segment | Time | Presenter |
  15|---|---|---|
  16| 1. Cold open + intro | 0:00 – 0:25 | **Jhon Clarence Rulona** |
  17| 2. Public browse | 0:25 – 0:55 | **Jovannie Mataganas** |
  18| 3. Seeker journey | 0:55 – 1:55 | **John Lienard Safuentes** |
  19| 4. Employer journey | 1:55 – 3:00 | **Cyril James Garnica** |
  20| 5. Admin journey | 3:00 – 4:00 | **Jahiel Tapiador** |
  21| 6. Tech, security, closing | 4:00 – 4:30 | **Jhon Clarence Rulona** |
  22
  23**Recording approach:** record each segment independently on the same machine, then stitch the clips end-to-end in Clipchamp (free, built into Windows) or any video editor. Each member only needs to be physically present for their own segment. The lead developer bookends the video for technical authority on the intro and closing.
  24
  25---
  26
  27## Pre-recording checklist
  28
  29- [ ] Run `php artisan migrate:fresh --seed` so demo data is fresh
  30- [ ] Run `php artisan serve` (port 8000) and confirm it's up
  31- [ ] Apache running for the frontend at `http://localhost/workbridge/`
  32- [ ] Hard refresh in the browser, DevTools closed
  33- [ ] Browser zoom at 100%
  34- [ ] Close unrelated tabs and notifications
  35- [ ] Have these credentials ready (paste into a notes window off-screen):
  36  - Seeker: `juan@example.com` / `password`
  37  - Employer: `hr@technova.ph` / `password`
  38  - Admin: `admin@workbridge.ph` / `password`
  39
  40---
  41
  42## 🎬 SCRIPT
  43
  44### [0:00 – 0:25] · Cold open
  45*Presenter: Jhon Clarence Rulona*
  46
  47**On screen:** Public homepage at `http://localhost/workbridge/`
  48
  49> "Good day, Sir/Ma'am. This is **WorkBridge PH** — a web-based unified employment platform built as our IT305 Major Course Output for Northwest Samar State University.
  50>
  51> The system addresses Sustainable Development Goal 8 — Decent Work and Economic Growth — by connecting job seekers, employers, and regulators in a single trusted platform that serves white-collar, blue-collar, and household workers alike."
  52
  53**Action:** Slowly scroll down the homepage so the viewer sees the hero, search, category tiles, and featured jobs.
  54
  55---
  56
  57### [0:25 – 0:55] · Public browse
  58*Presenter: Jovannie Mataganas*
  59
  60**On screen:** Click **Categories** in the top nav.
  61
  62> "The Categories page groups industries by sector — professional and office, healthcare and trades, and service and hospitality."
  63
  64**Action:** Click "Information Technology" → land on jobs filtered by IT.
  65
  66> "Filtered job listings stay readable. Notice the company avatars with verified-employer checkmarks, salary in proper peso format, and the live filter chip showing what's applied. A clear close button removes the filter cleanly."
  67
  68**Action:** Click X to clear filter. Click on any job → land on Job Detail page.
  69
  70> "Each job has its own detail page with overview, responsibilities, qualifications, and benefits tabs."
  71
  72---
  73
  74### [0:55 – 1:55] · Seeker journey *(60 sec — most important role to showcase)*
  75*Presenter: John Lienard Safuentes*
  76
  77**Action:** Top right → click **Log in** → use `juan@example.com` / `password`.
  78
  79> "Logging in as a job seeker — Juan dela Cruz, one of our seeded test users."
  80
  81**On screen:** Seeker dashboard.
  82
  83> "The dashboard shows saved jobs count, applications, and profile strength. Below are recommended jobs."
  84
  85**Action:** Click **Jobs** in sidebar → click Save (bookmark) on one job → click into another job → click **Apply Now** → confirm in modal.
  86
  87> "I can save jobs for later, and applying takes one click. The system warns me if I don't have a resume uploaded, calculates a skill-match score, and confirms before submission."
  88
  89**Action:** Click **Resume** in sidebar → click Download on the seeded resume.
  90
  91> "Resumes are stored privately on the server — only the seeker, the employer who received the application, and admins can download them."
  92
  93**Action:** Click **Profile** → **Change Photo** → pick a JPEG/PNG.
  94
  95> "Profile photos upload to a public storage area for display."
  96
  97**Action:** Click user dropdown top-right → Log out.
  98
  99---
 100
 101### [1:55 – 3:00] · Employer journey *(65 sec)*
 102*Presenter: Cyril James Garnica*
 103
 104**Action:** Log in as `hr@technova.ph` / `password`.
 105
 106> "Now as TechNova Solutions — an employer."
 107
 108**On screen:** Employer dashboard with charts.
 109
 110> "The employer dashboard surfaces four core metrics — active jobs, total applicants, shortlisted candidates, and hires — plus four live charts: applications pipeline, jobs by status, top-performing jobs, and a 30-day applications trend line. All driven by real data from a single backend query."
 111
 112**Action:** Click **Post Job** → fill in title, location, salary, description → click Post.
 113
 114> "Posting a job uses a live preview — the formatted salary updates as you type."
 115
 116**Action:** Click **Applicants** → click View on Juan's application.
 117
 118> "Reviewing an applicant — I can see their resume, skill-match score, and full profile."
 119
 120**Action:** Click **Reviewing** status button.
 121
 122> "Status changes write to an audit timeline and dispatch a notification to the seeker."
 123
 124**Action:** Logout.
 125
 126---
 127
 128### [3:00 – 4:00] · Admin journey *(60 sec)*
 129*Presenter: Jahiel Tapiador*
 130
 131**Action:** Log in as `admin@workbridge.ph` / `password`.
 132
 133**On screen:** Admin dashboard.
 134
 135> "As an admin, I have full platform oversight."
 136
 137**Action:** Click **Analytics** → show the 4 charts.
 138
 139> "Platform-wide analytics — users by role, companies by verification status, applications pipeline, and jobs by status. Each chart is a single SQL aggregation query on the backend."
 140
 141**Action:** Click **Employer Verification** → click any company.
 142
 143> "Admins verify or reject employer companies. Every decision writes to an audit log for accountability."
 144
 145**Action:** Click **Users** → demonstrate the ban/unban button on a non-admin user.
 146
 147> "Suspending or unbanning users — also audit-logged."
 148
 149**Action:** Click **Audit Logs**.
 150
 151> "Every administrative action is captured here with the actor, action type, target, and timestamp."
 152
 153---
 154
 155### [4:00 – 4:30] · Tech and security
 156*Presenter: Jhon Clarence Rulona (closing)*
 157
 158**On screen:** Briefly toggle dark mode in Settings.
 159
 160> "The platform supports a full dark mode across all pages, is fully mobile-responsive, and is built on a hardened technical foundation:
 161>
 162> Backend in **Laravel 11** with **MySQL 8** and **Sanctum** token authentication. Frontend in **vanilla JavaScript** with **Bootstrap 5** and **Chart.js**.
 163>
 164> Security includes role-based access control, rate limiting on authentication endpoints, XSS protection, IDOR prevention, and private file storage for sensitive uploads.
 165>
 166> Quality is enforced by **14 automated tests** — 7 PHPUnit feature tests on the backend and 7 Playwright end-to-end tests on the frontend.
 167>
 168> WorkBridge PH — connecting opportunity with capability, with integrity. Thank you."
 169
 170---
 171
 172## Post-recording
 173
 174- [ ] Trim dead air at the start and end
 175- [ ] Verify audio is clear (no background noise spikes)
 176- [ ] Export as MP4 (H.264, 1080p)
 177- [ ] File name: `WorkBridgePH_Demo_v1.mp4`
 178- [ ] Upload to MCO Google Drive folder `Project WorkBridge PH`
 179
 180## Tips for nailing the take
 181
 1821. **Speak in your normal voice but project slightly louder.** Phone-speaker playback is unforgiving.
 1832. **Don't apologize on camera** if you fumble — just pause, breathe, restart that segment.
 1843. **Mouse movements should be deliberate**, not frantic. Move, pause, click, pause.
 1854. **Highlight critical buttons by hovering 1 second** before clicking — gives the eye time to track.
 1865. **You don't have to memorize the script.** Tape it next to your camera or use teleprompter software (free: Teleprompter.io).
 187
 188You don't need to nail it in one take. Record in chunks per role, then stitch in any video editor (free: Clipchamp on Windows, DaVinci Resolve, or even Windows Photos).
 189
Documentation/capture_screenshots.js · 102 lines
   1/**
   2 * WorkBridge PH — Screenshot Capture Script
   3 * Captures all pages for MCO PowerPoint
   4 */
   5
   6const { chromium } = require('@playwright/test');
   7const path = require('path');
   8const fs = require('fs');
   9
  10const BASE = 'http://localhost:3000';
  11const OUT  = path.join(__dirname, 'screenshots');
  12
  13if (!fs.existsSync(OUT)) fs.mkdirSync(OUT);
  14
  15// Seed data injected for all protected pages
  16const SEED_SCRIPT = `
  17  if (!localStorage.getItem('workbridge_seeded')) {
  18    // minimal flag so app doesn't re-seed and wipe mock user
  19  }
  20`;
  21
  22const SEEKER_USER  = JSON.stringify({ id: 's1', email: 'juan@example.com',   name: 'Juan dela Cruz', role: 'seeker'   });
  23const EMPLOYER_USER= JSON.stringify({ id: 'c1', email: 'hr@technova.ph', name: 'TechNova Solutions HR', role: 'employer' });
  24const ADMIN_USER   = JSON.stringify({ id: 'admin1', email: 'admin@workbridge.ph', name: 'Admin WorkBridge', role: 'admin'   });
  25
  26const PAGES = [
  27  // ── Public ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  28  { file: '01_homepage',           url: '/',                              user: null,         label: 'Homepage' },
  29  { file: '02_jobs',               url: '/jobs.html',                     user: null,         label: 'Browse Jobs' },
  30  { file: '03_job_detail',         url: '/job-detail.html?id=j1',         user: null,         label: 'Job Detail' },
  31  { file: '04_companies',          url: '/companies.html',                user: null,         label: 'Companies' },
  32  { file: '05_company_detail',     url: '/company-detail.html?id=c1',     user: null,         label: 'Company Detail' },
  33  { file: '06_safety',             url: '/safety.html',                   user: null,         label: 'Safety & Awareness' },
  34  { file: '07_about',              url: '/about.html',                    user: null,         label: 'About' },
  35  { file: '08_help',               url: '/help.html',                     user: null,         label: 'Help' },
  36  // ── Auth ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  37  { file: '09_login',              url: '/auth/login.html',               user: null,         label: 'Login Page' },
  38  { file: '10_register',           url: '/auth/register.html',            user: null,         label: 'Register Page' },
  39  { file: '11_forgot',             url: '/auth/forgot.html',              user: null,         label: 'Forgot Password' },
  40  // ── Seeker ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  41  { file: '12_seeker_dashboard',   url: '/seeker/dashboard.html',         user: SEEKER_USER,  label: 'Seeker Dashboard' },
  42  { file: '13_seeker_jobs',        url: '/seeker/jobs.html',              user: SEEKER_USER,  label: 'Seeker — Browse Jobs' },
  43  { file: '14_seeker_saved',       url: '/seeker/saved-jobs.html',        user: SEEKER_USER,  label: 'Seeker — Saved Jobs' },
  44  { file: '15_seeker_applications',url: '/seeker/applications.html',      user: SEEKER_USER,  label: 'Seeker — Applications' },
  45  { file: '16_seeker_profile',     url: '/seeker/profile.html',           user: SEEKER_USER,  label: 'Seeker — Profile' },
  46  { file: '17_seeker_resume',      url: '/seeker/resume.html',            user: SEEKER_USER,  label: 'Seeker — Resume' },
  47  { file: '18_seeker_notifications',url: '/seeker/notifications.html',    user: SEEKER_USER,  label: 'Seeker — Notifications' },
  48  { file: '19_seeker_messages',    url: '/seeker/messages.html',          user: SEEKER_USER,  label: 'Seeker — Messages' },
  49  { file: '20_seeker_settings',    url: '/seeker/settings.html',          user: SEEKER_USER,  label: 'Seeker — Settings' },
  50  // ── Employer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  51  { file: '21_employer_dashboard', url: '/employer/dashboard.html',       user: EMPLOYER_USER,label: 'Employer Dashboard' },
  52  { file: '22_employer_post_job',  url: '/employer/post-job.html',        user: EMPLOYER_USER,label: 'Employer — Post Job' },
  53  { file: '23_employer_manage',    url: '/employer/manage-jobs.html',     user: EMPLOYER_USER,label: 'Employer — Manage Jobs' },
  54  { file: '24_employer_applicants',url: '/employer/applicants.html',      user: EMPLOYER_USER,label: 'Employer — Applicants' },
  55  { file: '25_employer_profile',   url: '/employer/company-profile.html', user: EMPLOYER_USER,label: 'Employer — Company Profile' },
  56  { file: '26_employer_verify',    url: '/employer/verification.html',    user: EMPLOYER_USER,label: 'Employer — Verification' },
  57  { file: '27_employer_messages',  url: '/employer/messages.html',        user: EMPLOYER_USER,label: 'Employer — Messages' },
  58  { file: '28_employer_settings',  url: '/employer/settings.html',        user: EMPLOYER_USER,label: 'Employer — Settings' },
  59  // ── Admin ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  60  { file: '29_admin_dashboard',    url: '/admin/dashboard.html',          user: ADMIN_USER,   label: 'Admin Dashboard' },
  61  { file: '30_admin_verification', url: '/admin/employer-verification.html', user: ADMIN_USER,label: 'Admin — Employer Verification' },
  62  { file: '31_admin_moderation',   url: '/admin/job-moderation.html',     user: ADMIN_USER,   label: 'Admin — Job Moderation' },
  63  { file: '32_admin_reports',      url: '/admin/reports.html',            user: ADMIN_USER,   label: 'Admin — Reports' },
  64  { file: '33_admin_users',        url: '/admin/users.html',              user: ADMIN_USER,   label: 'Admin — Users' },
  65  { file: '34_admin_analytics',    url: '/admin/analytics.html',          user: ADMIN_USER,   label: 'Admin — Analytics' },
  66  { file: '35_admin_categories',   url: '/admin/categories.html',         user: ADMIN_USER,   label: 'Admin — Categories' },
  67  { file: '36_admin_audit_logs',   url: '/admin/audit-logs.html',         user: ADMIN_USER,   label: 'Admin — Audit Logs' },
  68  { file: '37_admin_settings',     url: '/admin/settings.html',           user: ADMIN_USER,   label: 'Admin — Settings' },
  69];
  70
  71(async () => {
  72  const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true });
  73
  74  for (const page of PAGES) {
  75    const ctx = await browser.newContext({ viewport: { width: 1280, height: 800 } });
  76
  77    if (page.user) {
  78      await ctx.addInitScript(({ user }) => {
  79        localStorage.setItem('workbridge_user', user);
  80      }, { user: page.user });
  81    }
  82
  83    const p = await ctx.newPage();
  84
  85    try {
  86      await p.goto(BASE + page.url, { waitUntil: 'networkidle', timeout: 15000 });
  87      await p.waitForTimeout(800); // let animations settle
  88
  89      const filePath = path.join(OUT, page.file + '.png');
  90      await p.screenshot({ path: filePath, fullPage: false });
  91      console.log('✔ ' + page.label.padEnd(35) + page.file + '.png');
  92    } catch (err) {
  93      console.warn('✘ ' + page.label + ' — ' + err.message.split('\n')[0]);
  94    }
  95
  96    await ctx.close();
  97  }
  98
  99  await browser.close();
 100  console.log('\nDone. Screenshots saved to:', OUT);
 101})();
 102
Documentation/generate-code-bundle.js · 191 lines
   1#!/usr/bin/env node
   2/**
   3 * generate-code-bundle.js
   4 *
   5 * Bundles every source file in the project into a single printable HTML
   6 * (code-bundle.html) ready for IP / copyright submission.
   7 *
   8 * Usage:
   9 *   cd C:\Users\Admin\Downloads\Employment-main\Employment
  10 *   node Documentation/generate-code-bundle.js
  11 *
  12 * Then open code-bundle.html in Chrome/Edge → Ctrl+P → Save as PDF.
  13 *
  14 * The output includes a cover page, a table of contents, and every
  15 * source file with line numbers and file path headers.
  16 */
  17
  18const fs = require('fs');
  19const path = require('path');
  20
  21const ROOT   = process.cwd();
  22const OUTPUT = path.join(ROOT, 'code-bundle.html');
  23
  24// Directories we never descend into (mostly third-party code or build output)
  25const SKIP_DIRS = new Set([
  26  'node_modules', 'vendor', '.git', '.cursor', '.vscode', '.idea',
  27  'dist', 'build', 'coverage', 'test-results', 'playwright-report',
  28  'bootstrap', 'storage', 'public/build', 'public/hot', 'public/storage',
  29]);
  30
  31// File extensions we DO include (source code only)
  32const INCLUDE_EXT = new Set([
  33  '.html', '.htm', '.js', '.cjs', '.mjs',
  34  '.css', '.scss',
  35  '.php',
  36  '.json', '.md',
  37  '.env',
  38]);
  39
  40// Specific filenames to always include (no extension)
  41const INCLUDE_FILES = new Set(['.gitignore', '.env.example', '.env.production.example', 'serve.json']);
  42
  43const SKIP_FILES = new Set([
  44  'package-lock.json',  // 100k+ lines of dependency tree, irrelevant to IP
  45  'composer.lock',
  46  'code-bundle.html',   // don't include the output in itself
  47]);
  48
  49const MAX_FILE_BYTES = 500 * 1024; // skip anything > 500KB (binary-ish or generated)
  50
  51function walk(dir, out = []) {
  52  for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true })) {
  53    const full = path.join(dir, entry.name);
  54    const rel  = path.relative(ROOT, full).replace(/\\/g, '/');
  55
  56    if (entry.isDirectory()) {
  57      if (SKIP_DIRS.has(entry.name)) continue;
  58      // Belt-and-suspenders: also skip if any segment in the path is a skip dir
  59      if (rel.split('/').some(seg => SKIP_DIRS.has(seg))) continue;
  60      walk(full, out);
  61    } else if (entry.isFile()) {
  62      if (SKIP_FILES.has(entry.name)) continue;
  63      const ext = path.extname(entry.name).toLowerCase();
  64      if (INCLUDE_EXT.has(ext) || INCLUDE_FILES.has(entry.name)) {
  65        out.push(rel);
  66      }
  67    }
  68  }
  69  return out;
  70}
  71
  72function escapeHtml(s) {
  73  return s.replace(/[&<>]/g, c => ({ '&': '&amp;', '<': '&lt;', '>': '&gt;' }[c]));
  74}
  75
  76function isBinary(buf) {
  77  // Quick heuristic — null byte in the first 8KB means probably binary
  78  for (let i = 0; i < Math.min(buf.length, 8192); i++) if (buf[i] === 0) return true;
  79  return false;
  80}
  81
  82console.log('Scanning project tree…');
  83const files = walk(ROOT).sort();
  84console.log(`Found ${files.length} source files. Building bundle…`);
  85
  86const today = new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-US', { year: 'numeric', month: 'long', day: 'numeric' });
  87let totalLines = 0;
  88
  89const fileSections = [];
  90for (const rel of files) {
  91  const abs  = path.join(ROOT, rel);
  92  const stat = fs.statSync(abs);
  93  if (stat.size > MAX_FILE_BYTES) {
  94    console.warn(`  skipping (too large): ${rel}`);
  95    continue;
  96  }
  97  const buf = fs.readFileSync(abs);
  98  if (isBinary(buf)) {
  99    console.warn(`  skipping (binary):    ${rel}`);
 100    continue;
 101  }
 102  const content = buf.toString('utf8');
 103  const lines   = content.split('\n');
 104  totalLines   += lines.length;
 105
 106  const numbered = lines.map((line, i) =>
 107    `<span class="lineno">${String(i + 1).padStart(4)}</span>${escapeHtml(line)}`
 108  ).join('\n');
 109
 110  fileSections.push(`
 111<section class="file-section">
 112  <div class="file-header">${escapeHtml(rel)} <span class="file-meta">· ${lines.length} lines</span></div>
 113  <pre><code>${numbered}</code></pre>
 114</section>`);
 115}
 116
 117const tocItems = files.map(f => `<li>${escapeHtml(f)}</li>`).join('');
 118
 119const html = `<!DOCTYPE html>
 120<html lang="en">
 121<head>
 122<meta charset="utf-8">
 123<title>WorkBridge PH — Source Code Bundle</title>
 124<style>
 125  @page { size: letter; margin: 0.75in; }
 126  body { font: 10.5pt/1.4 Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif; color: #111; }
 127  h1   { font-size: 22pt; margin: 0 0 0.4em; }
 128  h2   { font-size: 13pt; margin: 1.4em 0 0.4em; padding-bottom: 0.3em; border-bottom: 2px solid #333; }
 129
 130  .cover { text-align: center; padding: 1.5in 0 1in; page-break-after: always; }
 131  .cover h1 { font-size: 30pt; letter-spacing: 0.5px; }
 132  .cover .subtitle { font-size: 14pt; color: #555; margin: 0.5em 0 2em; }
 133  .cover .meta { font-size: 11pt; line-height: 2; }
 134  .cover .meta strong { display: block; margin-top: 0.6em; }
 135
 136  .toc { page-break-after: always; }
 137  .toc-list { font-size: 9.5pt; line-height: 1.6; columns: 2; column-gap: 0.4in; }
 138  .toc-list li { break-inside: avoid; padding-left: 0.2em; }
 139
 140  .file-section { page-break-before: always; margin-top: 0; }
 141  .file-header  { background: #f0f0f0; padding: 6pt 10pt; border-left: 3px solid #444; font: 600 10pt 'Consolas', 'Courier New', monospace; margin-bottom: 4pt; }
 142  .file-meta    { color: #666; font-weight: 400; font-size: 9pt; }
 143
 144  pre  { background: #fafafa; border: 1px solid #ddd; padding: 6pt 8pt; overflow: visible; white-space: pre-wrap; word-break: break-all; margin: 0; }
 145  code { font: 8.5pt/1.45 'Consolas', 'Courier New', monospace; color: #1a1a1a; }
 146  .lineno { display: inline-block; width: 3.2em; color: #999; user-select: none; text-align: right; padding-right: 1em; }
 147
 148  @media print {
 149    body { margin: 0; }
 150    .file-section { page-break-before: always; }
 151  }
 152</style>
 153</head>
 154<body>
 155
 156<div class="cover">
 157  <h1>WorkBridge PH</h1>
 158  <p class="subtitle">A Web-Based Unified Employment Platform for Decent Work Access</p>
 159  <p class="meta">
 160    Major Course Output · IT305 Web Systems and Technologies<br>
 161    Northwest Samar State University · Calbayog City, Samar
 162    <strong>Authors</strong>
 163    Jhon Clarence Rulona<br>
 164    Jovannie Mataganas<br>
 165    John Lienard Safuentes<br>
 166    Cyril James Garnica<br>
 167    Jahiel Tapiador
 168    <strong>Submitted for Intellectual Property Protection</strong>
 169    ${today}
 170    <br>
 171    Source repository: github.com/SEPrimo19/workbridge-ph<br>
 172    Total source files: ${fileSections.length}<br>
 173    Total lines of code: ${totalLines.toLocaleString()}
 174  </p>
 175</div>
 176
 177<div class="toc">
 178  <h2>Table of Contents</h2>
 179  <ol class="toc-list">${tocItems}</ol>
 180</div>
 181
 182${fileSections.join('\n')}
 183
 184</body>
 185</html>`;
 186
 187fs.writeFileSync(OUTPUT, html);
 188console.log(`\n✓ Wrote ${OUTPUT}`);
 189console.log(`  ${fileSections.length} files · ${totalLines.toLocaleString()} lines · ${(html.length / 1024).toFixed(0)} KB`);
 190console.log(`\nNext: open code-bundle.html in Chrome → Ctrl+P → Save as PDF.`);
 191
README.md · 440 lines
   1# WorkBridge PH
   2
   3**Web-Based Unified Employment Platform for Decent Work Access**
   4*A Group 4 Capstone Project — Philippines*
   5
   6---
   7
   8## Project Overview
   9
  10WorkBridge PH is a globally accessible, web-based employment platform that connects job seekers and employers across all sectors — white-collar, blue-collar, and household/service workers. While the project originates from the Philippine context and uses it as its primary case study, the platform is designed to be accessible worldwide and addresses employment inequality problems that exist across all nations.
  11
  12The platform provides a centralized, safe, and inclusive job-matching ecosystem that bridges the gap between workers and employers regardless of geographic location.
  13
  14### Geographic Scope
  15
  16| Scope | Detail |
  17|-------|--------|
  18| **Accessibility** | Globally accessible via the internet |
  19| **Origin & Primary Context** | Philippines — used as the founding case study |
  20| **Target Users** | Job seekers and employers worldwide |
  21| **Platform Language** | English |
  22
  23### SDG Alignment
  24
  25The following Sustainable Development Goals are worldwide in scope. The problems this platform addresses are not unique to the Philippines — they are global challenges affecting workers in every country.
  26
  27| SDG | Goal | Global Problem Addressed |
  28|-----|------|--------------------------|
  29| **SDG 8** | Decent Work and Economic Growth | Hundreds of millions of workers worldwide lack access to decent, stable, and fairly compensated employment. Online job portals largely serve professional and white-collar workers, leaving blue-collar and service workers without structured digital recruitment. |
  30| **SDG 1** | No Poverty | Unemployment and underemployment are leading drivers of poverty globally. Improving access to employment opportunities is one of the most direct mechanisms for poverty reduction across all nations. |
  31| **SDG 10** | Reduced Inequalities | Digital recruitment platforms worldwide disproportionately serve educated, urban, and professional populations. Workers in the informal, household, and service sectors face systemic exclusion from these platforms, deepening inequality. |
  32
  33### Problem Being Solved
  34
  35These problems exist globally and are the basis for this platform:
  36
  37- Unequal access to digital recruitment platforms — blue-collar, household, and service workers worldwide are excluded
  38- Over-concentration of job portals on white-collar and professional careers
  39- Heavy reliance of informal-sector workers on unsafe, unverified recruitment channels
  40- Widespread exposure to job scams and fraudulent postings in all regions
  41- Workers in any country struggling to find verified, location-relevant opportunities
  42- Employers globally facing difficulty finding reliable and suitable workers efficiently
  43- Persistent job insecurity among informal and service workers across developing and developed economies
  44
  45---
  46
  47## Quick Start
  48
  491. **Run the Laravel API** (required for login and most dashboards):
  50
  51   ```bash
  52   cd backend
  53   php artisan serve
  54   ```
  55
  56   Default API URL is `http://localhost:8000` (matches `assets/js/api.js`).
  57
  582. **Serve the static HTML** on a different port (e.g. 3000). This repo includes `serve.json` with `"cleanUrls": false` so URLs like `job-detail.html?id=1` keep their query string (the default `serve` behavior otherwise drops `?id=` when rewriting to extensionless paths).
  59
  60   ```bash
  61   npm run serve
  62   # or: npx serve . -l 3000
  63   ```
  64
  65   Open `http://localhost:3000`. Do not use port 8000 for static files if the API is already on 8000.
  66
  673. **Demo accounts** — same as the login page and `DatabaseSeeder`; API must be running and the database migrated/seeded:
  68
  69   | Role | Email | Password |
  70   |------|-------|----------|
  71   | Job Seeker | `juan@example.com` | `password` |
  72   | Employer | `hr@technova.ph` | `password` |
  73   | Admin | `admin@workbridge.ph` | `password` |
  74
  75---
  76
  77## Features
  78
  79### Public Pages
  80| Page | Description |
  81|------|-------------|
  82| `index.html` | Hero search, featured jobs, browse by category, how it works |
  83| `jobs.html` | Search and filter by type, setup, salary, experience, location |
  84| `job-detail.html` | Overview, responsibilities, qualifications, benefits, apply/save |
  85| `companies.html` | Company directory, search, verified filter |
  86| `company-detail.html` | Company info, open job listings |
  87| `safety.html` | **Scam awareness and worker protection guidelines** |
  88| `about.html` | Platform purpose and objectives |
  89| `help.html` | FAQs and support |
  90| `terms.html` / `privacy.html` | Legal pages |
  91
  92### Authentication (`/auth/`)
  93- `login.html` — Demo login with role-based redirect
  94- `register.html` — Role selection (Seeker / Employer)
  95- `forgot.html` / `reset.html` — Password reset flow (simulated)
  96
  97### Job Seeker Dashboard (`/seeker/`)
  98- **Dashboard** — Stats: saved jobs, applications, profile strength, recommended jobs
  99- **Jobs** — Browse all listings with save and apply actions
 100- **Saved Jobs** — Manage saved listings
 101- **Applications** — Filter by status, view application timeline
 102- **Application Detail** — Timeline, notes, status tracking
 103- **Profile** — Editable profile sections with completion strength indicator
 104- **Resume** — Upload simulation with preview
 105- **Notifications** — Activity alerts
 106- **Messages** — Employer-seeker messaging (simulated)
 107- **Settings** — Account and preference management
 108
 109### Employer Dashboard (`/employer/`)
 110- **Dashboard** — KPI cards, applicant activity charts
 111- **Post Job** — Full job posting form with validation and preview
 112- **Manage Jobs** — Table view with edit/close actions
 113- **Applicants** — Per-job applicant list with status actions
 114- **Applicant Detail** — Resume preview, notes, shortlist/interview/reject actions
 115- **Company Profile** — Employer brand and info management
 116- **Verification** — Employer verification status and submission
 117- **Messages** — Communicate with applicants
 118- **Settings** — Account management
 119
 120### Admin Dashboard (`/admin/`)
 121- **Dashboard** — Pending queues: verifications, job reviews, reports
 122- **Employer Verification** — Review and verify/reject/suspend employer accounts
 123- **Job Moderation** — Approve or reject job postings, split-view review
 124- **Reports** — Scam/abuse report list and resolution workflow
 125- **Users** — Search, view, and suspend user accounts
 126- **Analytics** — Platform-wide stats, charts, top categories and locations
 127- **Categories** — CRUD management for job categories
 128- **Audit Logs** — Action history filtered by type and date
 129- **Settings** — Admin system configuration
 130
 131---
 132
 133## Project Structure
 134
 135```
 136Employment/
 137├── index.html                    # Public landing page
 138├── jobs.html                     # Public job search
 139├── job-detail.html               # Job detail view
 140├── companies.html                # Company directory
 141├── company-detail.html           # Company detail view

 236
 237---
 238
 239## Framework & Technology Stack
 240
 241WorkBridge PH is built in two distinct phases. The current state is a complete frontend prototype. The target state is a full-stack application powered by Laravel and MySQL.
 242
 243---
 244
 245### Phase 1 — Frontend Prototype (Current)
 246
 247| Layer | Technology | Version | Purpose |
 248|-------|-----------|---------|---------|
 249| Markup | HTML5 | — | Page structure and content |
 250| Styling | CSS3 Custom Properties | — | Design tokens, layout, theming |
 251| Scripting | Vanilla JavaScript (ES Modules) | ES2020+ | Application logic, data handling |
 252| UI Framework | Bootstrap | 5.3.2 | Responsive grid, utility classes |
 253| Icon Library | Bootstrap Icons | 1.11.1 | UI iconography |
 254| Data / State | Browser `localStorage` | — | Mock backend; simulates database |
 255| Routing | Custom client-side router | — | Role-based guards, query params |
 256| End-to-End Testing | Playwright | `@playwright/test ^1.58.2` | Browser automation tests |
 257
 258---
 259
 260### Phase 2 — Full-Stack Target (Backend Integration)
 261
 262#### Backend
 263
 264| Layer | Technology | Version | Purpose |
 265|-------|-----------|---------|---------|
 266| Language | PHP | 8.2+ | Server-side scripting |
 267| Framework | Laravel | 11.x | MVC application framework |
 268| Authentication | Laravel Sanctum | (bundled) | API token authentication for SPA/MPA |
 269| ORM | Laravel Eloquent | (bundled) | Database models and relationships |
 270| Validation | Laravel Form Requests | (bundled) | Server-side input validation |
 271| Authorization | Laravel Gates & Policies | (bundled) | Per-resource role and ownership checks |
 272| File Storage | Laravel Storage (local / S3) | (bundled) | Resume and document uploads |
 273| Events & Listeners | Laravel Events | (bundled) | Audit logging, notification triggers |
 274| Mail | Laravel Mail + SMTP | (bundled) | Email verification, password reset, job alerts |
 275| Queue | Laravel Queue | (bundled) | Background jobs (email, notifications) |
 276| Dependency Manager | Composer | 2.x | PHP package management |
 277
 278#### Database
 279
 280| Layer | Technology | Version | Purpose |
 281|-------|-----------|---------|---------|
 282| Database Engine | MySQL | 8.0+ | Primary relational data store |
 283| Migrations | Laravel Migrations | (bundled) | Version-controlled schema management |
 284| Seeders | Laravel Seeders | (bundled) | Converts `data.seed.js` mock data to SQL |
 285| Query Builder | Laravel Eloquent / Query Builder | (bundled) | Server-side filtering, search, pagination |
 286
 287#### Development Environment
 288
 289| Tool | Technology | Purpose |
 290|------|-----------|---------|
 291| Local Server | XAMPP (Apache + MySQL + PHP) | Local development stack for Windows |
 292| Alternative | Laravel Herd or Laravel Sail (Docker) | Cross-platform alternative |
 293| Package Manager | Composer | PHP dependencies |
 294| Package Manager | npm | Frontend test tooling |
 295| Version Control | Git | Source control |
 296
 297#### Testing (Full-Stack)
 298
 299| Tool | Technology | Purpose |
 300|------|-----------|---------|
 301| Backend Unit Tests | PHPUnit (via Laravel) | Test individual classes and methods |
 302| Feature / Integration Tests | Laravel HTTP Tests | Test API endpoints end-to-end |
 303| Frontend E2E Tests | Playwright | Browser-level user flow testing |
 304
 305---
 306
 307### Architecture Pattern
 308
 309| Pattern | Description |
 310|---------|-------------|
 311| **Multi-Page Application (MPA)** | Each page is a standalone `.html` file; no frontend SPA framework |
 312| **REST API** | Laravel exposes JSON API endpoints; frontend consumes via `fetch()` |
 313| **MVC (Model-View-Controller)** | Laravel separates data (Eloquent models), logic (controllers/services), and output (JSON responses) |
 314| **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)** | Three roles — `seeker`, `employer`, `admin` — enforced by Laravel middleware and Policies |
 315| **Token Authentication** | Laravel Sanctum issues API tokens stored in `localStorage`; sent as `Authorization: Bearer` headers |
 316| **Token-based Design System** | All colors, spacing, and typography managed via CSS custom properties in `tokens.css` |
 317| **Module-based JS** | ES module imports with role-specific logic split into separate `.js` modules |
 318
 319---
 320
 321### Why Laravel?
 322
 323| Reason | Detail |
 324|--------|--------|
 325| PHP ecosystem fit | Runs on standard shared hosting (Apache + PHP) available globally, including affordable options in developing countries |
 326| Built-in auth scaffolding | Sanctum handles API token auth with minimal boilerplate |
 327| Eloquent ORM | Clean, expressive database access; maps directly to the existing data models |
 328| Form Requests | Centralized, reusable validation — replaces fragile HTML5-only validation |
 329| Policies & Gates | Enforces ownership (employer edits only their own jobs; seeker views own applications) |
 330| Laravel Storage | Handles resume/document uploads to local disk or cloud (S3) without extra libraries |
 331| Active ecosystem | Large community, long-term LTS support, extensive documentation |
 332| Artisan CLI | Scaffolding, migrations, seeders, and testing all managed from one command interface |
 333
 334---
 335
 336### Why MySQL?
 337
 338| Reason | Detail |
 339|--------|--------|
 340| Relational data model | Jobs, companies, seekers, applications, and reports all have well-defined relationships |
 341| Laravel Eloquent compatibility | First-class MySQL support; migrations and seeders work out of the box |
 342| Industry standard | Widely available on hosting providers globally |
 343| Performance | Indexing on `job_id`, `seeker_id`, `status`, `location` for efficient filtered queries |
 344| XAMPP bundled | Available immediately in local dev environment without extra setup |
 345
 346### Global Accessibility Considerations
 347
 348Because the platform is accessible worldwide, the following must be accounted for in design and backend integration:
 349
 350| Consideration | Approach |
 351|--------------|----------|
 352| **Currency** | Display salary in the currency relevant to the job posting location; default to PHP (₱) for Philippine-origin listings |
 353| **Location** | Free-text location field supporting any city, region, or country — not restricted to Philippine geography |
 354| **Phone Numbers** | Support international format (+country code) |
 355| **Employer Verification** | Accept business registration documents from any country |
 356| **Language** | English as the platform language; content submitted by users may vary |
 357| **Time Zones** | Store all timestamps in UTC; display in user's local time |
 358
 359---
 360
 361## Seed Data
 362
 363Managed in `assets/js/data.seed.js`, auto-loaded on first visit:
 364
 365| Entity | Count |
 366|--------|-------|
 367| Jobs | 24 |
 368| Companies | 10 |
 369| Applications | 12 |
 370| Reports | 8 |
 371| Audit Logs | 20 |
 372

 375## Proposal Alignment
 376
 377| Proposal Requirement | Status | Notes |
 378|---------------------|--------|-------|

 380| Home page — platform overview & featured jobs | ✅ Complete | `index.html` |
 381| Browse Jobs — search and filter listings | ✅ Complete | `jobs.html` |
 382| Job Categories — white/blue-collar, household | ⚠️ Partial | Homepage has category section; no dedicated public `/categories.html` page yet |
 383| Safety & Awareness — scam prevention | ✅ Complete | `safety.html` |
 384| About Page | ✅ Complete | `about.html` |
 385| Job Seeker — Profile Management | ✅ Complete | `seeker/profile.html` |
 386| Job Seeker — Saved Jobs | ✅ Complete | `seeker/saved-jobs.html` |
 387| Job Seeker — Applications & Status Tracking | ✅ Complete | `seeker/applications.html` + detail |
 388| Employer — Employer Profile | ✅ Complete | `employer/company-profile.html` |
 389| Employer — Job Posting and Management | ✅ Complete | `employer/post-job.html` + `manage-jobs.html` |
 390| Employer — Applicant Review | ✅ Complete | `employer/applicants.html` + detail |
 391| Admin — Employer Verification | ✅ Complete | `admin/employer-verification.html` |
 392| Admin — Job Post Monitoring | ✅ Complete | `admin/job-moderation.html` |
 393| Admin — Report and Scam Management | ✅ Complete | `admin/reports.html` |
 394| Admin — System Analytics | ✅ Complete | `admin/analytics.html` |
 395| Employment & livelihood guidance materials | ⚠️ Partial | `help.html` covers guidance; no dedicated livelihood resources page |
 396| Location-based job opportunities | ⚠️ Partial | Location filter exists in UI; no real geolocation integration |
 397| Employer profiles and verification status | ✅ Complete | Verification badge system implemented |
 398| Job application records and status | ✅ Complete | Full application lifecycle with status tracking |
 399
 400---
 401
 402## Running Tests
 403
 404```bash
 405# Install dependencies
 406npm install
 407
 408# Start local server first
 409npx serve . &
 410
 411# Run Playwright tests
 412npx playwright test
 413```
 414
 415---
 416
 417## Next Phase: Backend Integration
 418
 419The current prototype is Phase 1 (Frontend). See [ROADMAP.md](ROADMAP.md) for the full development plan.
 420
 421To convert to a full-stack application:
 4221. Replace `localStorage` store with **MySQL** via **Laravel Eloquent**
 4232. Build REST API endpoints using **Laravel** controllers
 4243. Implement real authentication using **Laravel Sanctum** (API token-based)
 4254. Convert `data.seed.js` into **Laravel Seeders** with SQL migrations
 4265. Replace client-side filtering with server-side **Eloquent** queries
 4276. Implement real file upload using **Laravel Storage**
 428
 429---
 430

 432

ROADMAP.md · 539 lines
   1# WorkBridge PH — Project Roadmap & Progress Tracking
   2
   3**Web-Based Unified Employment Platform for Decent Work Access**
   4*Group 4 Capstone Project*
   5
   6---
   7
   8## Overview
   9
  10This document tracks the full development roadmap of WorkBridge PH from prototype to production-ready platform. Progress is updated as each milestone is completed.
  11
  12**Current Phase:** Phase 1.5 — Bug Fixes & Finalization
  13**Overall Progress:** Phase 1 Complete · Phase 1.5 In Progress · Phase 2 Not Started
  14
  15### Real Deadlines
  16
  17| Deadline | Requirement |
  18|----------|-------------|
  19| **April 22, 2026** | Fully functional system (frontend, all flows working) |
  20| **April 23, 2026** | **MIDTERM PRESENTATION** — live demo of system |
  21| **May 10, 2026** | Laravel + MySQL backend complete, deployed |
  22| **May 15, 2026** | 25 users tested, feedback collected, 2 faculty evaluations |
  23| **May 16+, 2026** | Lobby tarpaulin posting |
  24
  25### Platform Scope
  26
  27WorkBridge PH is **globally accessible** via the internet. While it originates from a Philippine context and uses it as the primary case study, it addresses worldwide problems aligned with **SDG 8 (Decent Work), SDG 1 (No Poverty), and SDG 10 (Reduced Inequalities)** — all of which are global in nature. The platform is open to job seekers and employers from any country.
  28
  29**Architectural implications of global scope:**
  30- Location fields are free-text and not restricted to Philippine geography
  31- Currency must be flexible per job posting (default PHP ₱ for PH-origin listings)
  32- Phone numbers must support international format (+country code)
  33- All timestamps stored in UTC
  34- Employer verification accepts documents from any country
  35
  36---
  37
  38## Phase Summary
  39
  40| Phase | Name | Status | Deadline | Description |
  41|-------|------|--------|----------|-------------|
  42| **1** | Frontend Prototype | ✅ Complete | Done | All UI pages built; localStorage mock backend |
  43| **1.5** | Bug Fixes & Finalization | 🚧 In Progress | April 22 | Fix all bugs, make fully functional for midterm |
  44| **2** | Backend Integration | 🔲 Not Started | May 10 | Laravel + MySQL API, replace localStorage |
  45| **3** | Deploy & User Testing | 🔲 Not Started | May 15 | Deploy, 25 users, feedback forms, faculty eval |
  46| **4** | Lobby Posting | 🔲 Not Started | May 16+ | Tarpaulin with system info, tech stack, QR code |
  47

 152---
 153
 154## Phase 2 — Backend Integration (Laravel + MySQL)
 155
 156**Goal:** Replace `localStorage` with a Laravel REST API backed by MySQL. The frontend HTML pages remain; `store.js` is replaced by `api.js` that calls Laravel endpoints via `fetch()`.
 157**Stack:** PHP 8.2+, Laravel 11.x, Laravel Sanctum, Eloquent ORM, MySQL 8.0+
 158**Status:** 🔲 Not Started
 159
 160---
 161
 162### 2.1 — Environment Setup
 163
 164| Task | Status | Notes |
 165|------|--------|-------|
 166| Install XAMPP (Apache + MySQL 8.0 + PHP 8.2) | 🔲 Todo | Primary local dev environment on Windows |
 167| Install Composer (PHP dependency manager) | 🔲 Todo | Required to create Laravel project |
 168| Create Laravel 11 project inside repo | 🔲 Todo | `composer create-project laravel/laravel backend` |
 169| Install Laravel Sanctum | 🔲 Todo | `php artisan install:api` — API token authentication |
 170| Configure `.env` — database credentials, app URL | 🔲 Todo | Never commit `.env` to version control |
 171| Configure Apache virtual host for Laravel | 🔲 Todo | Point document root to `backend/public/` |
 172| Enable CORS for frontend origin | 🔲 Todo | Configure `config/cors.php` in Laravel |
 173| Verify `php artisan serve` runs without errors | 🔲 Todo | |
 174
 175---
 176
 177### 2.2 — Database Schema & Migrations
 178
 179| Task | Status | Notes |
 180|------|--------|-------|
 181| Design final MySQL schema | 🔲 Todo | See schema below |
 182| Create migration: `users` | 🔲 Todo | id, name, email, password, role, email_verified_at, created_at |
 183| Create migration: `seekers` | 🔲 Todo | user_id (FK), location, country, phone, profile_strength |
 184| Create migration: `seeker_skills` | 🔲 Todo | seeker_id (FK), skill — pivot-style |
 185| Create migration: `companies` | 🔲 Todo | id, user_id (FK), name, industry, size, location, country, verified_status, website, about, logo_path, created_at |
 186| Create migration: `company_contacts` | 🔲 Todo | company_id (FK), email, phone |
 187| Create migration: `jobs` | 🔲 Todo | id, company_id (FK), title, location, country, job_type, setup, salary_min, salary_max, salary_currency, experience_level, description, requirements, benefits, status, posted_at, closed_at |
 188| Create migration: `job_tags` | 🔲 Todo | job_id (FK), tag |
 189| Create migration: `applications` | 🔲 Todo | id, job_id (FK), seeker_id (FK), status, notes, applied_at |
 190| Create migration: `application_timeline` | 🔲 Todo | id, application_id (FK), event, note, created_at |
 191| Create migration: `saved_jobs` | 🔲 Todo | seeker_id (FK), job_id (FK), saved_at — composite PK |
 192| Create migration: `categories` | 🔲 Todo | id, name, icon, created_at |
 193| Create migration: `reports` | 🔲 Todo | id, type, target_id, reason, severity, status, reporter_id (FK), resolution_note, created_at, resolved_at |
 194| Create migration: `audit_logs` | 🔲 Todo | id, actor_id (FK), action, target_id, created_at |
 195| Create migration: `notifications` | 🔲 Todo | id, user_id (FK), title, message, read_at, created_at |
 196| Create migration: `messages` | 🔲 Todo | id, sender_id (FK), recipient_id (FK), content, read_at, created_at |
 197| Create migration: `resumes` | 🔲 Todo | id, seeker_id (FK), file_path, original_name, uploaded_at |
 198| Create migration: `verification_documents` | 🔲 Todo | id, company_id (FK), file_path, uploaded_at |
 199| Add DB indexes on frequently filtered columns | 🔲 Todo | `status`, `location`, `job_type`, `company_id`, `seeker_id` |
 200| Run all migrations: `php artisan migrate` | 🔲 Todo | |
 201

 204### 2.3 — Eloquent Models
 205
 206| Task | Status | Notes |
 207|------|--------|-------|

 212| `Application` model — belongs to Job and Seeker, has many timeline events | 🔲 Todo | |

 218| `Resume` model | 🔲 Todo | |

 222### 2.4 — Seeders
 223
 224| Task | Status | Notes |
 225|------|--------|-------|

 227| Convert `data.seed.js` companies to `CompanySeeder` | 🔲 Todo | 10 companies |

 237
 238### 2.5 — Authentication (Laravel Sanctum)
 239
 240| Task | Status | Notes |
 241|------|--------|-------|
 242| `POST /api/auth/register` — create user, issue token | 🔲 Todo | Hash password; create Seeker or Company record by role |
 243| `POST /api/auth/login` — validate credentials, issue token | 🔲 Todo | Return token + user object |
 244| `POST /api/auth/logout` — revoke current token | 🔲 Todo | `$request->user()->currentAccessToken()->delete()` |
 245| `GET /api/auth/me` — return authenticated user | 🔲 Todo | Used by frontend to restore session |
 246| `POST /api/auth/forgot-password` — send reset link | 🔲 Todo | Laravel built-in password broker |
 247| `POST /api/auth/reset-password` — apply new password | 🔲 Todo | |
 248| `POST /api/auth/verify-email` — confirm email address | 🔲 Todo | |
 249| Sanctum middleware on all protected routes | 🔲 Todo | `auth:sanctum` middleware group |
 250| Role middleware — `EnsureRole` — blocks wrong-role access | 🔲 Todo | Custom middleware: checks `user->role` |
 251

 260| `/api/auth/login` | POST | — | 🔲 Todo |
 261| `/api/auth/logout` | POST | `auth:sanctum` | 🔲 Todo |
 262| `/api/auth/me` | GET | `auth:sanctum` | 🔲 Todo |
 263| `/api/auth/forgot-password` | POST | — | 🔲 Todo |
 264| `/api/auth/reset-password` | POST | — | 🔲 Todo |
 265
 266#### Jobs
 267| Endpoint | Method | Middleware | Status |
 268|----------|--------|-----------|--------|
 269| `/api/jobs` | GET | — | 🔲 Todo |
 270| `/api/jobs/{id}` | GET | — | 🔲 Todo |
 271| `/api/jobs` | POST | `auth:sanctum`, `role:employer` | 🔲 Todo |
 272| `/api/jobs/{id}` | PATCH | `auth:sanctum`, `role:employer`, owns job | 🔲 Todo |
 273| `/api/jobs/{id}` | DELETE | `auth:sanctum`, `role:employer`, owns job | 🔲 Todo |

 276#### Companies

 280| `/api/companies/{id}` | GET | — | 🔲 Todo |
 281| `/api/companies/{id}` | PATCH | `auth:sanctum`, `role:employer`, owns company | 🔲 Todo |

 283#### Applications

 342### 2.8 — Authorization (Laravel Policies)
 343
 344| Task | Status | Notes |
 345|------|--------|-------|
 346| `JobPolicy` — employer can only edit/delete their own jobs | 🔲 Todo | |
 347| `ApplicationPolicy` — seeker views own; employer views apps for their jobs | 🔲 Todo | |
 348| `CompanyPolicy` — employer can only update their own company | 🔲 Todo | |
 349| `UserPolicy` — admin can suspend; user can edit own profile | 🔲 Todo | |
 350| Register all policies in `AuthServiceProvider` | 🔲 Todo | |

 408## Phase 3 — Advanced Features
 409
 410**Goal:** Enhance the platform with real-time capabilities and smart matching.
 411**Status:** 🔲 Not Started
 412
 413| Feature | Status | Notes |
 414|---------|--------|-------|
 415| Real-time messaging (WebSockets or polling) | 🔲 Todo | Replace simulated messages |
 416| Email notifications (job matches, application updates) | 🔲 Todo | SMTP integration |
 417| Geolocation-based job search | 🔲 Todo | Browser Geolocation API + server-side distance filtering |
 418| Skill-based job matching algorithm | 🔲 Todo | Match seeker skills to job requirements |
 419| Job alert subscriptions | 🔲 Todo | Notify seekers of new matching jobs |
 420| Advanced resume builder | 🔲 Todo | Replace upload-only simulation |
 421| Employer analytics dashboard (real data) | 🔲 Todo | Charts driven by actual application data |
 422| Social sharing for job listings | 🔲 Todo | |
 423| Mobile responsive improvements | 🔲 Todo | Test and refine on actual mobile devices |

 440| Feature test: `GET /api/jobs` — pagination, filters | 🔲 Todo | |
 441| Feature test: `POST /api/jobs` — employer only | 🔲 Todo | Auth, role enforcement |
 442| Feature test: job ownership — employer can't edit another's job | 🔲 Todo | Policy test |
 443| Feature test: `POST /api/jobs/{id}/apply` — seeker only | 🔲 Todo | |
 444| Feature test: admin endpoints — non-admin gets 403 | 🔲 Todo | |
 445| Feature test: file upload validation | 🔲 Todo | Wrong type, over size limit |

 454| Test: register as employer → post job → view applicant | 🔲 Todo | Full employer flow |

 457| Test: search jobs by keyword and location | 🔲 Todo | |
 458| Test: profile edit persists after page refresh | 🔲 Todo | |
 459| Test: unauthorized access redirects to login | 🔲 Todo | Existing test covers this |
