  16BCRYPT_ROUNDS=12
  17
  18LOG_CHANNEL=stack
  19LOG_STACK=single
  20LOG_DEPRECATIONS_CHANNEL=null

  22
  23DB_CONNECTION=sqlite

  26# DB_DATABASE=laravel
  27# DB_USERNAME=root
  28# DB_PASSWORD=

  32SESSION_ENCRYPT=false
  33SESSION_PATH=/
  34SESSION_DOMAIN=null
  35
  36BROADCAST_CONNECTION=log
  37FILESYSTEM_DISK=local
  38QUEUE_CONNECTION=database
  39
  40CACHE_STORE=database
  41# CACHE_PREFIX=
  42

  51MAIL_SCHEME=null

  54MAIL_USERNAME=null
  55MAIL_PASSWORD=null

  60AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=
  61AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=us-east-1
  62AWS_BUCKET=
  63AWS_USE_PATH_STYLE_ENDPOINT=false
  64
  65VITE_APP_NAME="${APP_NAME}"

backend/.env.production.example · 49 lines
   1##############################################################
   2# WorkBridge PH — PRODUCTION environment template
   3# Copy to .env on the server and fill in real values.
   4# NEVER commit the real .env file to git.
   5##############################################################
   6
   7APP_NAME="WorkBridge PH"
   8APP_ENV=production
   9APP_KEY=                        # Run: php artisan key:generate
  10APP_DEBUG=false                 # MUST be false in production
  11APP_URL=https://your-domain.com
  12
  13APP_LOCALE=en
  14APP_FALLBACK_LOCALE=en
  15
  16BCRYPT_ROUNDS=12
  17
  18LOG_CHANNEL=stack
  19LOG_STACK=single
  20LOG_LEVEL=error                 # Only log errors in production
  21
  22DB_CONNECTION=mysql
  23DB_HOST=127.0.0.1
  24DB_PORT=3306
  25DB_DATABASE=workbridge
  26DB_USERNAME=workbridge_user     # Use a dedicated DB user, NOT root
  27DB_PASSWORD=CHANGE_ME_STRONG_PASSWORD
  28
  29SESSION_DRIVER=database
  30SESSION_LIFETIME=120
  31SESSION_ENCRYPT=true            # Encrypt sessions in production
  32SESSION_PATH=/
  33SESSION_DOMAIN=your-domain.com
  34
  35SANCTUM_STATEFUL_DOMAINS=your-domain.com
  36FRONTEND_URL=https://your-domain.com
  37
  38FILESYSTEM_DISK=local
  39QUEUE_CONNECTION=database
  40CACHE_STORE=database
  41
  42MAIL_MAILER=smtp
  43MAIL_HOST=smtp.your-provider.com
  44MAIL_PORT=587
  45MAIL_USERNAME=your@email.com
  46MAIL_PASSWORD=CHANGE_ME
  47MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS="noreply@your-domain.com"
  48MAIL_FROM_NAME="WorkBridge PH"
  49

backend/README.md · 60 lines
   1<p align="center"><a href="https://laravel.com" target="_blank"><img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/laravel/art/master/logo-lockup/5%20SVG/2%20CMYK/1%20Full%20Color/laravel-logolockup-cmyk-red.svg" width="400" alt="Laravel Logo"></a></p>
   2
   3<p align="center">
   4<a href="https://github.com/laravel/framework/actions"><img src="https://github.com/laravel/framework/workflows/tests/badge.svg" alt="Build Status"></a>
   5<a href="https://packagist.org/packages/laravel/framework"><img src="https://img.shields.io/packagist/dt/laravel/framework" alt="Total Downloads"></a>
   6<a href="https://packagist.org/packages/laravel/framework"><img src="https://img.shields.io/packagist/v/laravel/framework" alt="Latest Stable Version"></a>
   7<a href="https://packagist.org/packages/laravel/framework"><img src="https://img.shields.io/packagist/l/laravel/framework" alt="License"></a>
   8</p>
   9
  10## About Laravel
  11
  12Laravel is a web application framework with expressive, elegant syntax. We believe development must be an enjoyable and creative experience to be truly fulfilling. Laravel takes the pain out of development by easing common tasks used in many web projects, such as:
  13
  14- [Simple, fast routing engine](https://laravel.com/docs/routing).
  15- [Powerful dependency injection container](https://laravel.com/docs/container).
  16- Multiple back-ends for [session](https://laravel.com/docs/session) and [cache](https://laravel.com/docs/cache) storage.
  17- Expressive, intuitive [database ORM](https://laravel.com/docs/eloquent).
  18- Database agnostic [schema migrations](https://laravel.com/docs/migrations).
  19- [Robust background job processing](https://laravel.com/docs/queues).
  20- [Real-time event broadcasting](https://laravel.com/docs/broadcasting).
  21
  22Laravel is accessible, powerful, and provides tools required for large, robust applications.
  23
  24## Learning Laravel
  25
  26Laravel has the most extensive and thorough [documentation](https://laravel.com/docs) and video tutorial library of all modern web application frameworks, making it a breeze to get started with the framework. You can also check out [Laravel Learn](https://laravel.com/learn), where you will be guided through building a modern Laravel application.
  27
  28If you don't feel like reading, [Laracasts](https://laracasts.com) can help. Laracasts contains thousands of video tutorials on a range of topics including Laravel, modern PHP, unit testing, and JavaScript. Boost your skills by digging into our comprehensive video library.
  29
  30## Laravel Sponsors
  31
  32We would like to extend our thanks to the following sponsors for funding Laravel development. If you are interested in becoming a sponsor, please visit the [Laravel Partners program](https://partners.laravel.com).
  33
  34### Premium Partners
  35
  36- **[Vehikl](https://vehikl.com)**
  37- **[Tighten Co.](https://tighten.co)**
  38- **[Kirschbaum Development Group](https://kirschbaumdevelopment.com)**
  39- **[64 Robots](https://64robots.com)**
  40- **[Curotec](https://www.curotec.com/services/technologies/laravel)**
  41- **[DevSquad](https://devsquad.com/hire-laravel-developers)**
  42- **[Redberry](https://redberry.international/laravel-development)**
  43- **[Active Logic](https://activelogic.com)**
  44
  45## Contributing
  46
  47Thank you for considering contributing to the Laravel framework! The contribution guide can be found in the [Laravel documentation](https://laravel.com/docs/contributions).
  48
  49## Code of Conduct
  50
  51In order to ensure that the Laravel community is welcoming to all, please review and abide by the [Code of Conduct](https://laravel.com/docs/contributions#code-of-conduct).
  52
  53## Security Vulnerabilities
  54
  55If you discover a security vulnerability within Laravel, please send an e-mail to Taylor Otwell via [taylor@laravel.com](mailto:taylor@laravel.com). All security vulnerabilities will be promptly addressed.
  56
  57## License
  58
  59The Laravel framework is open-sourced software licensed under the [MIT license](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).
  60
backend/app/Http/Controllers/Api/AdminController.php · 218 lines
   1<?php
   2
   3namespace App\Http\Controllers\Api;
   4
   5use App\Http\Controllers\Controller;
   6use App\Models\Application;
   7use App\Models\AuditLog;
   8use App\Models\Category;
   9use App\Models\Company;
  10use App\Models\JobListing;
  11use App\Models\Report;
  12use App\Models\User;
  13use Illuminate\Http\Request;
  14use Illuminate\Support\Facades\DB;
  15use Illuminate\Support\Str;
  16
  17class AdminController extends Controller
  18{
  19    public function dashboard(Request $request)
  20    {
  21        return response()->json([
  22            // Top-line counters (existing — used by stat cards)
  23            'total_users'     => User::count(),
  24            'total_jobs'      => JobListing::count(),
  25            'open_jobs'       => JobListing::where('status', 'Open')->count(),
  26            'total_companies' => Company::count(),
  27            'pending_verif'   => Company::where('verified_status', 'Pending')->count(),
  28            'open_reports'    => Report::where('status', 'Open')->count(),
  29
  30            // Breakdowns for charts. groupBy returns Collection<string, count>.
  31            'users_by_role'             => User::select('role', DB::raw('count(*) as count'))
  32                                              ->groupBy('role')->pluck('count', 'role'),
  33            'jobs_by_status'            => JobListing::select('status', DB::raw('count(*) as count'))
  34                                              ->groupBy('status')->pluck('count', 'status'),
  35            'applications_by_status'    => Application::select('status', DB::raw('count(*) as count'))
  36                                              ->groupBy('status')->pluck('count', 'status'),
  37            'companies_by_verification' => Company::select('verified_status', DB::raw('count(*) as count'))
  38                                              ->groupBy('verified_status')->pluck('count', 'verified_status'),
  39        ]);
  40    }
  41
  42    public function users(Request $request)
  43    {
  44        $query = User::query();
  45
  46        if ($role = $request->query('role')) {
  47            $query->where('role', $role);
  48        }
  49
  50        if ($status = $request->query('status')) {
  51            $query->where('status', $status);
  52        }
  53
  54        if ($search = $request->query('search')) {
  55            $query->where(function ($q) use ($search) {
  56                $q->where('name', 'like', "%$search%")
  57                  ->orWhere('email', 'like', "%$search%");
  58            });
  59        }
  60
  61        return response()->json($query->latest()->paginate(20));
  62    }
  63
  64    public function banUser(Request $request, $userId)
  65    {
  66        $user = User::findOrFail($userId);
  67
  68        if ($user->role === 'admin') {
  69            return response()->json(['message' => 'Cannot ban an admin.'], 403);
  70        }
  71
  72        $user->update(['status' => 'banned']);
  73        $this->audit($request, 'ban_user', 'user', $userId);
  74
  75        return response()->json(['message' => 'User banned.']);
  76    }
  77
  78    public function unbanUser(Request $request, $userId)
  79    {
  80        $user = User::findOrFail($userId);
  81        $user->update(['status' => 'active']);
  82        $this->audit($request, 'unban_user', 'user', $userId);
  83
  84        return response()->json(['message' => 'User unbanned.']);
  85    }
  86
  87    public function pendingVerifications(Request $request)
  88    {
  89        // Return all companies so the admin can review history (Verified/Rejected/Suspended too)
  90        // and filter client-side. The original "pending only" filter was misnamed for the use case.
  91        $query = Company::with(['user', 'verificationDocuments'])->latest();
  92
  93        if ($status = $request->query('status')) {
  94            $query->where('verified_status', $status);
  95        }
  96
  97        return response()->json($query->paginate(50));
  98    }
  99
 100    public function verifyCompany(Request $request, $companyId)
 101    {
 102        $data = $request->validate([
 103            'status' => 'required|in:Verified,Rejected,Suspended',
 104            'note'   => 'nullable|string|max:500',
 105        ]);
 106
 107        $company = Company::findOrFail($companyId);
 108        $company->update([
 109            'verified_status' => $data['status'],
 110            'verified_at'     => $data['status'] === 'Verified' ? now() : null,
 111        ]);
 112
 113        $this->audit($request, 'verify_company:' . $data['status'], 'company', $companyId);
 114
 115        return response()->json($company->fresh());
 116    }
 117
 118    public function reports(Request $request)
 119    {
 120        $query = Report::with('reporter');
 121
 122        if ($status = $request->query('status')) {
 123            $query->where('status', $status);
 124        }
 125
 126        return response()->json($query->latest()->paginate(20));
 127    }
 128
 129    public function resolveReport(Request $request, $reportId)
 130    {
 131        $data = $request->validate([
 132            'status' => 'required|in:Resolved,Dismissed',
 133            'note'   => 'nullable|string|max:500',
 134        ]);
 135
 136        $report = Report::findOrFail($reportId);
 137        $report->update([
 138            'status'      => $data['status'],
 139            'resolved_by' => $request->user()->id,
 140            'resolved_at' => now(),
 141        ]);
 142
 143        $this->audit($request, 'resolve_report:' . $data['status'], 'report', $reportId);
 144
 145        return response()->json($report->fresh());
 146    }
 147
 148    public function allJobs(Request $request)
 149    {
 150        $jobs = JobListing::with(['company', 'category'])
 151            ->withCount('applications')
 152            ->latest()
 153            ->paginate(20);
 154
 155        return response()->json($jobs);
 156    }
 157
 158    public function takedownJob(Request $request, $jobId)
 159    {
 160        $job = JobListing::findOrFail($jobId);
 161        $job->update(['status' => 'Closed']);
 162        $this->audit($request, 'takedown_job', 'job_listing', $jobId);
 163
 164        return response()->json(['message' => 'Job listing closed.']);
 165    }
 166
 167    public function auditLogs(Request $request)
 168    {
 169        $logs = AuditLog::with('actor')->latest('created_at')->paginate(50);
 170        return response()->json($logs);
 171    }
 172
 173    public function approveJob(Request $request, $jobId)
 174    {
 175        $job = JobListing::findOrFail($jobId);
 176        $job->update(['status' => 'Open']);
 177        $this->audit($request, 'approve_job', 'job_listing', $jobId);
 178
 179        return response()->json(['message' => 'Job approved.']);
 180    }
 181
 182    public function categories(Request $request)
 183    {
 184        return response()->json(Category::withCount('jobListings')->get());
 185    }
 186
 187    public function addCategory(Request $request)
 188    {
 189        $data = $request->validate(['name' => 'required|string|max:100|unique:categories,name']);
 190        $cat = Category::create(['name' => $data['name'], 'slug' => Str::slug($data['name'])]);
 191        $this->audit($request, 'add_category', 'category', $cat->id);
 192
 193        return response()->json($cat, 201);
 194    }
 195
 196    public function deleteCategory(Request $request, $id)
 197    {
 198        $cat = Category::findOrFail($id);
 199        $cat->delete();
 200        $this->audit($request, 'delete_category', 'category', $id);
 201
 202        return response()->json(['message' => 'Deleted.']);
 203    }
 204
 205    private function audit(Request $request, string $action, string $targetType, int $targetId): void
 206    {
 207        $user = $request->user();
 208        AuditLog::create([
 209            'actor_id'    => $user->id,
 210            'actor_email' => $user->email,
 211            'action'      => $action,
 212            'target_type' => $targetType,
 213            'target_id'   => $targetId,
 214            'ip_address'  => $request->ip(),
 215        ]);
 216    }
 217}

backend/app/Http/Controllers/Api/ApplicationController.php · 172 lines
   1<?php
   2
   3namespace App\Http\Controllers\Api;
   4
   5use App\Http\Controllers\Controller;
   6use App\Models\Application;
   7use App\Models\ApplicationTimeline;
   8use App\Models\JobListing;
   9use App\Models\WbNotification;
  10use Illuminate\Http\Request;
  11
  12class ApplicationController extends Controller
  13{
  14    public function apply(Request $request, $jobId)
  15    {
  16        $user = $request->user();
  17        $seeker = $user->seeker;
  18
  19        if (! $seeker) {
  20            return response()->json(['message' => 'Seeker profile not found.'], 403);
  21        }
  22
  23        $job = JobListing::findOrFail($jobId);
  24
  25        if ($job->status !== 'Open') {
  26            return response()->json(['message' => 'This job is no longer accepting applications.'], 422);
  27        }
  28
  29        $existing = Application::where('job_listing_id', $jobId)
  30            ->where('seeker_id', $seeker->id)
  31            ->first();
  32
  33        if ($existing) {
  34            return response()->json(['message' => 'You have already applied to this job.'], 422);
  35        }
  36
  37        $data = $request->validate([
  38            'resume_id'    => 'nullable|exists:resumes,id',
  39            'cover_letter' => 'nullable|string|max:2000',
  40            'skill_fit'    => 'nullable|integer|min:0|max:100',
  41        ]);
  42
  43        $app = Application::create([
  44            'job_listing_id' => $jobId,
  45            'seeker_id'      => $seeker->id,
  46            'resume_id'      => $data['resume_id'] ?? null,
  47            'cover_letter'   => $data['cover_letter'] ?? null,
  48            'skill_fit'      => $data['skill_fit'] ?? null,
  49            'status'         => 'Pending',
  50        ]);
  51
  52        ApplicationTimeline::create([
  53            'application_id' => $app->id,
  54            'status'         => 'Pending',
  55            'note'           => 'Application submitted.',
  56            'changed_by'     => $user->id,
  57        ]);
  58
  59        WbNotification::create([
  60            'user_id' => $job->company->user_id,
  61            'type'    => 'new_application',
  62            'title'   => 'New Application',
  63            'body'    => "{$user->name} applied to \"{$job->title}\".",
  64            'link'    => "/employer/applicants.html",
  65        ]);
  66
  67        return response()->json($app->load('timelines'), 201);
  68    }
  69
  70    public function myApplications(Request $request)
  71    {
  72        $seeker = $request->user()->seeker;
  73
  74        if (! $seeker) {
  75            return response()->json(['data' => []]);
  76        }
  77
  78        $apps = Application::with(['jobListing.company', 'jobListing.tags', 'timelines'])
  79            ->where('seeker_id', $seeker->id)
  80            ->latest()
  81            ->paginate(20);
  82
  83        return response()->json($apps);
  84    }
  85
  86    public function forJob(Request $request, $jobId)
  87    {
  88        $user = $request->user();
  89        $company = $user->company;
  90
  91        $job = JobListing::findOrFail($jobId);
  92
  93        if ($company && $job->company_id !== $company->id && $user->role !== 'admin') {
  94            return response()->json(['message' => 'Forbidden.'], 403);
  95        }
  96
  97        $apps = Application::with(['seeker.user', 'seeker.skills', 'resume', 'timelines'])
  98            ->where('job_listing_id', $jobId)
  99            ->latest()
 100            ->paginate(20);
 101
 102        return response()->json($apps);
 103    }
 104
 105    public function updateStatus(Request $request, $appId)
 106    {
 107        $user = $request->user();
 108        $app = Application::with('jobListing.company')->findOrFail($appId);
 109
 110        if ($app->jobListing->company->user_id !== $user->id && $user->role !== 'admin') {
 111            return response()->json(['message' => 'Forbidden.'], 403);
 112        }
 113
 114        $data = $request->validate([
 115            'status' => 'required|in:Pending,Reviewing,Interview,Hired,Rejected',
 116            'note'   => 'nullable|string|max:500',
 117        ]);
 118
 119        $app->update(['status' => $data['status']]);
 120
 121        ApplicationTimeline::create([
 122            'application_id' => $app->id,
 123            'status'         => $data['status'],
 124            'note'           => $data['note'] ?? null,
 125            'changed_by'     => $user->id,
 126        ]);
 127
 128        WbNotification::create([
 129            'user_id' => $app->seeker->user_id,
 130            'type'    => 'application_status',
 131            'title'   => 'Application Update',
 132            'body'    => "Your application for \"{$app->jobListing->title}\" is now {$data['status']}.",
 133            'link'    => "/seeker/applications.html",
 134        ]);
 135
 136        return response()->json($app->load('timelines'));
 137    }
 138
 139    public function show(Request $request, $appId)
 140    {
 141        $user = $request->user();
 142        $app = Application::with(['jobListing.company', 'jobListing.tags', 'seeker.user', 'seeker.skills', 'timelines'])->findOrFail($appId);
 143
 144        $isOwner = $app->seeker?->user_id === $user->id;
 145        $isEmployer = $app->jobListing->company?->user_id === $user->id;
 146
 147        if (! $isOwner && ! $isEmployer && $user->role !== 'admin') {
 148            return response()->json(['message' => 'Forbidden.'], 403);
 149        }
 150
 151        return response()->json($app);
 152    }
 153
 154    public function withdraw(Request $request, $appId)
 155    {
 156        $user = $request->user();
 157        $seeker = $user->seeker;
 158        $app = Application::findOrFail($appId);
 159
 160        if ($app->seeker_id !== $seeker?->id) {
 161            return response()->json(['message' => 'Forbidden.'], 403);
 162        }
 163
 164        if (in_array($app->status, ['Hired', 'Rejected'])) {
 165            return response()->json(['message' => 'Cannot withdraw a finalized application.'], 422);
 166        }
 167
 168        $app->delete();
 169        return response()->json(['message' => 'Application withdrawn.']);
 170    }
 171}
 172
backend/app/Http/Controllers/Api/AuthController.php · 204 lines
   1<?php
   2
   3namespace App\Http\Controllers\Api;
   4
   5use App\Http\Controllers\Controller;
   6use App\Models\Company;
   7use App\Models\Seeker;
   8use App\Models\User;
   9use Illuminate\Http\Request;
  10use Illuminate\Support\Carbon;
  11use Illuminate\Support\Facades\DB;
  12use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Hash;
  13use Illuminate\Support\Str;
  14use Illuminate\Validation\ValidationException;
  15
  16class AuthController extends Controller
  17{
  18    public function register(Request $request)
  19    {
  20        $data = $request->validate([
  21            'name'     => 'required|string|max:255',
  22            'email'    => 'required|email|unique:users,email',
  23            'password' => 'required|string|min:6|confirmed',
  24            'role'     => 'required|in:seeker,employer',
  25        ]);
  26
  27        $user = User::create([
  28            'name'     => $data['name'],
  29            'email'    => $data['email'],
  30            'password' => $data['password'],
  31            'role'     => $data['role'],
  32            'status'   => 'active',
  33        ]);
  34
  35        if ($data['role'] === 'seeker') {
  36            Seeker::create(['user_id' => $user->id]);
  37        } elseif ($data['role'] === 'employer') {
  38            $companyName = $request->input('company_name', $data['name'] . "'s Company");
  39            Company::create([
  40                'user_id'         => $user->id,
  41                'name'            => $companyName,
  42                'slug'            => \Illuminate\Support\Str::slug($companyName) . '-' . $user->id,
  43                'verified_status' => 'Unverified',
  44            ]);
  45        }
  46
  47        $token = $user->createToken('auth_token')->plainTextToken;
  48
  49        return response()->json([
  50            'user'  => $this->userPayload($user),
  51            'token' => $token,
  52        ], 201);
  53    }
  54
  55    public function login(Request $request)
  56    {
  57        $data = $request->validate([
  58            'email'    => 'required|email',
  59            'password' => 'required|string',
  60        ]);
  61
  62        $user = User::where('email', $data['email'])->first();
  63
  64        if (! $user || ! Hash::check($data['password'], $user->password)) {
  65            throw ValidationException::withMessages([
  66                'email' => ['The provided credentials are incorrect.'],
  67            ]);
  68        }
  69
  70        if ($user->status === 'banned') {
  71            return response()->json(['message' => 'Your account has been banned.'], 403);
  72        }
  73
  74        $token = $user->createToken('auth_token')->plainTextToken;
  75
  76        return response()->json([
  77            'user'  => $this->userPayload($user),
  78            'token' => $token,
  79        ]);
  80    }
  81
  82    public function logout(Request $request)
  83    {
  84        $request->user()->currentAccessToken()->delete();
  85        return response()->json(['message' => 'Logged out.']);
  86    }
  87
  88    public function me(Request $request)
  89    {
  90        return response()->json($this->userPayload($request->user()));
  91    }
  92
  93    /**
  94     * Issue a one-time password reset token. Stores a hashed token in
  95     * password_reset_tokens (Laravel's default scaffolding). The email
  96     * existence is not leaked — same response either way.
  97     *
  98     * Demo mode: since SMTP isn't configured, the reset URL is returned in
  99     * the JSON response so the frontend can display it. In production this
 100     * field is removed and the URL is emailed instead.
 101     */
 102    public function forgotPassword(Request $request)
 103    {
 104        $data = $request->validate(['email' => 'required|email']);
 105
 106        $user = User::where('email', $data['email'])->first();
 107        $response = ['message' => 'If that email is registered, a reset link has been sent.'];
 108
 109        if ($user) {
 110            $token = Str::random(64);
 111            DB::table('password_reset_tokens')->updateOrInsert(
 112                ['email' => $data['email']],
 113                [
 114                    'email'      => $data['email'],
 115                    'token'      => Hash::make($token),
 116                    'created_at' => now(),
 117                ]
 118            );
 119
 120            $frontend = config('app.frontend_url') ?: 'http://localhost/workbridge';
 121            $response['demo_reset_url'] = $frontend . '/auth/reset.html?token=' . $token
 122                . '&email=' . urlencode($data['email']);
 123        }
 124
 125        return response()->json($response);
 126    }
 127
 128    /**
 129     * Verify the reset token and update the password. Tokens expire after
 130     * 60 minutes and are single-use (deleted on success).
 131     */
 132    public function resetPassword(Request $request)
 133    {
 134        $data = $request->validate([
 135            'email'    => 'required|email',
 136            'token'    => 'required|string',
 137            'password' => 'required|string|min:6|confirmed',
 138        ]);
 139
 140        $row = DB::table('password_reset_tokens')->where('email', $data['email'])->first();
 141
 142        if (! $row || ! Hash::check($data['token'], $row->token)) {
 143            return response()->json(['message' => 'Invalid or expired reset link.'], 422);
 144        }
 145
 146        if (Carbon::parse($row->created_at)->diffInMinutes(now()) > 60) {
 147            DB::table('password_reset_tokens')->where('email', $data['email'])->delete();
 148            return response()->json(['message' => 'Reset link has expired. Request a new one.'], 422);
 149        }
 150
 151        $user = User::where('email', $data['email'])->first();
 152        if (! $user) {
 153            return response()->json(['message' => 'Invalid or expired reset link.'], 422);
 154        }
 155
 156        $user->update(['password' => Hash::make($data['password'])]);
 157
 158        // Invalidate the token (single use) and revoke any existing API tokens
 159        // so anyone with stolen credentials is logged out.
 160        DB::table('password_reset_tokens')->where('email', $data['email'])->delete();
 161        $user->tokens()->delete();
 162
 163        return response()->json(['message' => 'Password reset successful.']);
 164    }
 165
 166    public function changePassword(Request $request)
 167    {
 168        $data = $request->validate([
 169            'current_password' => 'required|string',
 170            'new_password'     => 'required|string|min:6|different:current_password',
 171        ]);
 172
 173        $user = $request->user();
 174
 175        if (! Hash::check($data['current_password'], $user->password)) {
 176            return response()->json(['message' => 'Current password is incorrect.'], 422);
 177        }
 178
 179        $user->update(['password' => Hash::make($data['new_password'])]);
 180
 181        return response()->json(['message' => 'Password updated.']);
 182    }
 183
 184    private function userPayload(User $user): array
 185    {
 186        $payload = [
 187            'id'    => $user->id,
 188            'name'  => $user->name,
 189            'email' => $user->email,
 190            'role'  => $user->role,
 191            'status' => $user->status,
 192        ];
 193
 194        if ($user->role === 'seeker') {
 195            $seeker = $user->seeker()->with('skills')->first();
 196            $payload['seeker'] = $seeker;
 197        } elseif ($user->role === 'employer') {
 198            $payload['company'] = $user->company;
 199        }
 200
 201        return $payload;
 202    }
 203}
 204
backend/app/Http/Controllers/Api/CompanyController.php · 104 lines
   1<?php
   2
   3namespace App\Http\Controllers\Api;
   4
   5use App\Http\Controllers\Controller;
   6use App\Models\Company;
   7use App\Models\VerificationDocument;
   8use Illuminate\Http\Request;
   9use Illuminate\Support\Str;
  10
  11class CompanyController extends Controller
  12{
  13    public function index(Request $request)
  14    {
  15        $query = Company::with('user')->where('verified_status', 'Verified');
  16
  17        if ($search = $request->query('search')) {
  18            $query->where('name', 'like', "%$search%")
  19                  ->orWhere('industry', 'like', "%$search%");
  20        }
  21
  22        return response()->json($query->latest()->paginate(15));
  23    }
  24
  25    public function show($id)
  26    {
  27        $company = Company::with(['user', 'jobListings' => function ($q) {
  28            $q->where('status', 'Open')->with('tags')->latest()->limit(10);
  29        }])->findOrFail($id);
  30
  31        return response()->json($company);
  32    }
  33
  34    public function myCompany(Request $request)
  35    {
  36        $company = $request->user()->company()->with('verificationDocuments')->first();
  37        return response()->json($company);
  38    }
  39
  40    public function updateCompany(Request $request)
  41    {
  42        $user = $request->user();
  43        $company = $user->company;
  44
  45        if (! $company) {
  46            return response()->json(['message' => 'No company profile found.'], 404);
  47        }
  48
  49        $data = $request->validate([
  50            'name'          => 'sometimes|string|max:255',
  51            'industry'      => 'nullable|string|max:100',
  52            'size'          => 'nullable|string|max:50',
  53            'founded_year'  => 'nullable|integer|min:1800|max:2100',
  54            'website'       => 'nullable|url|max:255',
  55            'description'   => 'nullable|string|max:3000',
  56            'address'       => 'nullable|string|max:255',
  57            'city'          => 'nullable|string|max:100',
  58            'province'      => 'nullable|string|max:100',
  59        ]);
  60
  61        if (isset($data['name'])) {
  62            $data['slug'] = Str::slug($data['name']) . '-' . $company->id;
  63        }
  64
  65        $company->update($data);
  66
  67        return response()->json($company->fresh());
  68    }
  69
  70    public function uploadLogo(Request $request)
  71    {
  72        $request->validate(['logo' => 'required|image|max:2048']);
  73        $path = $request->file('logo')->store('logos', 'public');
  74        $request->user()->company->update(['logo' => $path]);
  75        return response()->json(['logo' => $path]);
  76    }
  77
  78    public function uploadVerificationDoc(Request $request)
  79    {
  80        $request->validate([
  81            'document' => 'required|file|mimes:pdf,jpg,jpeg,png|max:5120',
  82            'doc_type' => 'required|string|max:100',
  83        ]);
  84
  85        $company = $request->user()->company;
  86
  87        if (! $company) {
  88            return response()->json(['message' => 'No company profile.'], 403);
  89        }
  90
  91        $file = $request->file('document');
  92        $path = $file->store('verification_docs', 'public');
  93
  94        $doc = VerificationDocument::create([
  95            'company_id'    => $company->id,
  96            'file_path'     => $path,
  97            'original_name' => $file->getClientOriginalName(),
  98            'doc_type'      => $request->input('doc_type'),
  99        ]);
 100
 101        return response()->json($doc, 201);
 102    }
 103}
 104
backend/app/Http/Controllers/Api/JobController.php · 246 lines
   1<?php
   2
   3namespace App\Http\Controllers\Api;
   4
   5use App\Http\Controllers\Controller;
   6use App\Models\Application;
   7use App\Models\JobListing;
   8use App\Models\JobTag;
   9use Illuminate\Http\Request;
  10use Illuminate\Support\Facades\DB;
  11
  12class JobController extends Controller
  13{
  14    public function index(Request $request)
  15    {
  16        $query = JobListing::with(['company', 'tags', 'category'])
  17            ->where('status', 'Open');
  18
  19        if ($search = $request->query('search')) {
  20            $query->where(function ($q) use ($search) {
  21                $q->where('title', 'like', "%$search%")
  22                  ->orWhere('location', 'like', "%$search%")
  23                  ->orWhere('description', 'like', "%$search%");
  24            });
  25        }
  26
  27        if ($type = $request->query('type')) {
  28            $query->where('type', $type);
  29        }
  30
  31        if ($location = $request->query('location')) {
  32            $query->where('location', 'like', "%$location%");
  33        }
  34
  35        if ($category = $request->query('category_id')) {
  36            $query->where('category_id', $category);
  37        }
  38
  39        if ($experience = $request->query('experience_level')) {
  40            $query->where('experience_level', $experience);
  41        }
  42
  43        $jobs = $query->latest()->paginate(15);
  44
  45        return response()->json($jobs);
  46    }
  47
  48    public function show($id)
  49    {
  50        $job = JobListing::with(['company', 'tags', 'category'])->findOrFail($id);
  51        return response()->json($job);
  52    }
  53
  54    public function store(Request $request)
  55    {
  56        $user = $request->user();
  57        $company = $user->company;
  58
  59        if (! $company) {
  60            return response()->json(['message' => 'No company profile found.'], 403);
  61        }
  62
  63        $data = $request->validate([
  64            'title'            => 'required|string|max:255',
  65            'description'      => 'required|string',
  66            'requirements'     => 'nullable|string',
  67            'location'         => 'required|string|max:255',
  68            'type'             => 'required|in:Full-time,Part-time,Contract,Internship,Freelance',
  69            'experience_level' => 'required|in:Entry-level,Mid-level,Senior,Executive',
  70            'salary_min'       => 'nullable|numeric|min:0',
  71            'salary_max'       => 'nullable|numeric|min:0',
  72            'slots'            => 'nullable|integer|min:1',
  73            'deadline'         => 'nullable|date',
  74            'category_id'      => 'nullable|exists:categories,id',
  75            'tags'             => 'nullable|array',
  76            'tags.*'           => 'string|max:50',
  77        ]);
  78
  79        $job = JobListing::create([
  80            ...$data,
  81            'company_id' => $company->id,
  82            'status'     => 'Open',
  83        ]);
  84
  85        if (! empty($data['tags'])) {
  86            foreach ($data['tags'] as $tag) {
  87                JobTag::create(['job_listing_id' => $job->id, 'tag' => $tag]);
  88            }
  89        }
  90
  91        return response()->json($job->load(['tags', 'category']), 201);
  92    }
  93
  94    public function update(Request $request, $id)
  95    {
  96        $user = $request->user();
  97        $job = JobListing::findOrFail($id);
  98
  99        if ($job->company->user_id !== $user->id) {
 100            return response()->json(['message' => 'Forbidden.'], 403);
 101        }
 102
 103        $data = $request->validate([
 104            'title'            => 'sometimes|string|max:255',
 105            'description'      => 'sometimes|string',
 106            'requirements'     => 'nullable|string',
 107            'location'         => 'sometimes|string|max:255',
 108            'type'             => 'sometimes|in:Full-time,Part-time,Contract,Internship,Freelance',
 109            'experience_level' => 'sometimes|in:Entry-level,Mid-level,Senior,Executive',
 110            'salary_min'       => 'nullable|numeric|min:0',
 111            'salary_max'       => 'nullable|numeric|min:0',
 112            'slots'            => 'nullable|integer|min:1',
 113            'deadline'         => 'nullable|date',
 114            'status'           => 'sometimes|in:Open,Closed,Draft',
 115            'category_id'      => 'nullable|exists:categories,id',
 116            'tags'             => 'nullable|array',
 117            'tags.*'           => 'string|max:50',
 118        ]);
 119
 120        $job->update($data);
 121
 122        if (isset($data['tags'])) {
 123            $job->tags()->delete();
 124            foreach ($data['tags'] as $tag) {
 125                JobTag::create(['job_listing_id' => $job->id, 'tag' => $tag]);
 126            }
 127        }
 128
 129        return response()->json($job->load(['tags', 'category']));
 130    }
 131
 132    public function destroy(Request $request, $id)
 133    {
 134        $user = $request->user();
 135        $job = JobListing::findOrFail($id);
 136
 137        if ($job->company->user_id !== $user->id && $user->role !== 'admin') {
 138            return response()->json(['message' => 'Forbidden.'], 403);
 139        }
 140
 141        $job->delete();
 142        return response()->json(['message' => 'Job listing deleted.']);
 143    }
 144
 145    public function byCompany(Request $request, $companyId)
 146    {
 147        $jobs = JobListing::with(['tags', 'category'])
 148            ->where('company_id', $companyId)
 149            ->latest()
 150            ->paginate(15);
 151
 152        return response()->json($jobs);
 153    }
 154
 155    public function myJobs(Request $request)
 156    {
 157        $company = $request->user()->company;
 158
 159        if (! $company) {
 160            return response()->json(['data' => []]);
 161        }
 162
 163        $jobs = JobListing::with(['tags', 'category'])
 164            ->where('company_id', $company->id)
 165            ->withCount('applications')
 166            ->latest()
 167            ->paginate(20);
 168
 169        return response()->json($jobs);
 170    }
 171
 172    /**
 173     * Aggregated dashboard data for the logged-in employer.
 174     * Returns 4 totals + 4 chart datasets in one call so the frontend
 175     * doesn't spam the API on page load.
 176     */
 177    public function employerDashboard(Request $request)
 178    {
 179        $company = $request->user()->company;
 180
 181        // Defensive: an employer without a company record (shouldn't happen post-register, but…)
 182        if (! $company) {
 183            return response()->json([
 184                'totals' => ['active_jobs' => 0, 'total_applicants' => 0, 'shortlisted' => 0, 'hires' => 0],
 185                'applications_by_status'    => (object) [],
 186                'jobs_by_status'            => (object) [],
 187                'top_jobs'                  => [],
 188                'applications_last_30_days' => (object) [],
 189            ]);
 190        }
 191
 192        $companyId = $company->id;
 193        $jobIds    = JobListing::where('company_id', $companyId)->pluck('id');
 194
 195        // Top-line counters (used by the 4 stat cards)
 196        $activeJobs      = JobListing::where('company_id', $companyId)->where('status', 'Open')->count();
 197        $totalApplicants = Application::whereIn('job_listing_id', $jobIds)->count();
 198        $shortlisted     = Application::whereIn('job_listing_id', $jobIds)
 199                              ->whereIn('status', ['Reviewing', 'Interview'])->count();
 200        $hires           = Application::whereIn('job_listing_id', $jobIds)
 201                              ->where('status', 'Hired')->count();
 202
 203        // Pipeline donut: applications grouped by status
 204        $appsByStatus = Application::whereIn('job_listing_id', $jobIds)
 205            ->select('status', DB::raw('count(*) as count'))
 206            ->groupBy('status')->pluck('count', 'status');
 207
 208        // Jobs by status (Open / Draft / Closed) — bar chart
 209        $jobsByStatus = JobListing::where('company_id', $companyId)
 210            ->select('status', DB::raw('count(*) as count'))
 211            ->groupBy('status')->pluck('count', 'status');
 212
 213        // Top 5 jobs by applicant volume — horizontal bar chart
 214        $topJobs = JobListing::where('company_id', $companyId)
 215            ->withCount('applications')
 216            ->orderByDesc('applications_count')
 217            ->limit(5)
 218            ->get(['id', 'title']);
 219
 220        // Applications over the last 30 days — line chart time series.
 221        // We fill in zero-count days client-side so the line is continuous.
 222        $thirtyDaysAgo = now()->subDays(29)->startOfDay();
 223        $appsLast30 = Application::whereIn('job_listing_id', $jobIds)
 224            ->where('created_at', '>=', $thirtyDaysAgo)
 225            ->select(DB::raw('DATE(created_at) as date'), DB::raw('count(*) as count'))
 226            ->groupBy('date')->pluck('count', 'date');
 227
 228        return response()->json([
 229            'totals' => [
 230                'active_jobs'      => $activeJobs,
 231                'total_applicants' => $totalApplicants,
 232                'shortlisted'      => $shortlisted,
 233                'hires'            => $hires,
 234            ],
 235            'applications_by_status'    => $appsByStatus,
 236            'jobs_by_status'            => $jobsByStatus,
 237            'top_jobs'                  => $topJobs->map(fn ($j) => [
 238                'id'                 => $j->id,
 239                'title'              => $j->title,
 240                'applications_count' => $j->applications_count,
 241            ]),
 242            'applications_last_30_days' => $appsLast30,
 243        ]);
 244    }
 245}
 246
backend/app/Http/Controllers/Api/MessageController.php · 104 lines
   1<?php
   2
   3namespace App\Http\Controllers\Api;
   4
   5use App\Http\Controllers\Controller;
   6use App\Models\Message;
   7use App\Models\User;
   8use Illuminate\Http\Request;
   9
  10class MessageController extends Controller
  11{
  12    // Roles that can communicate with each other
  13    private const ALLOWED_PAIRS = [
  14        'seeker'   => ['employer', 'admin'],
  15        'employer' => ['seeker', 'admin'],
  16        'admin'    => ['seeker', 'employer'],
  17    ];
  18
  19    public function contacts(Request $request)
  20    {
  21        $userId = $request->user()->id;
  22
  23        $contactIds = Message::where('sender_id', $userId)
  24            ->orWhere('recipient_id', $userId)
  25            ->get()
  26            ->flatMap(fn (Message $m) => [$m->sender_id, $m->recipient_id])
  27            ->unique()
  28            ->filter(fn ($id) => $id !== $userId)
  29            ->values();
  30
  31        $contacts = User::whereIn('id', $contactIds)->get(['id', 'name', 'email', 'role']);
  32
  33        return response()->json($contacts);
  34    }
  35
  36    public function thread(Request $request, $otherUserId)
  37    {
  38        $user   = $request->user();
  39        $userId = $user->id;
  40
  41        // Verify the other user exists and the role pair is allowed
  42        $other = User::findOrFail($otherUserId);
  43        $allowed = self::ALLOWED_PAIRS[$user->role] ?? [];
  44
  45        if (! in_array($other->role, $allowed)) {
  46            return response()->json(['message' => 'You cannot view messages with this user.'], 403);
  47        }
  48
  49        $messages = Message::where(function ($q) use ($userId, $otherUserId) {
  50            $q->where('sender_id', $userId)->where('recipient_id', $otherUserId);
  51        })->orWhere(function ($q) use ($userId, $otherUserId) {
  52            $q->where('sender_id', $otherUserId)->where('recipient_id', $userId);
  53        })->oldest()->get();
  54
  55        // Mark incoming messages as read
  56        Message::where('sender_id', $otherUserId)
  57            ->where('recipient_id', $userId)
  58            ->where('is_read', false)
  59            ->update(['is_read' => true]);
  60
  61        return response()->json($messages);
  62    }
  63
  64    public function send(Request $request)
  65    {
  66        $data = $request->validate([
  67            'recipient_id' => 'required|exists:users,id',
  68            'body'         => 'required|string|max:2000',
  69        ]);
  70
  71        $sender    = $request->user();
  72        $recipient = User::findOrFail($data['recipient_id']);
  73
  74        // Prevent messaging yourself
  75        if ($sender->id === $recipient->id) {
  76            return response()->json(['message' => 'You cannot message yourself.'], 422);
  77        }
  78
  79        // Enforce cross-role messaging only
  80        $allowed = self::ALLOWED_PAIRS[$sender->role] ?? [];
  81        if (! in_array($recipient->role, $allowed)) {
  82            return response()->json(['message' => 'You can only message employers or admins.'], 403);
  83        }
  84
  85        $message = Message::create([
  86            'sender_id'    => $sender->id,
  87            'recipient_id' => $recipient->id,
  88            'body'         => $data['body'],
  89            'is_read'      => false,
  90        ]);
  91
  92        return response()->json($message, 201);
  93    }
  94
  95    public function unreadCount(Request $request)
  96    {
  97        $count = Message::where('recipient_id', $request->user()->id)
  98            ->where('is_read', false)
  99            ->count();
 100
 101        return response()->json(['unread' => $count]);
 102    }
 103}
 104
backend/app/Http/Controllers/Api/NotificationController.php · 47 lines
   1<?php
   2
   3namespace App\Http\Controllers\Api;
   4
   5use App\Http\Controllers\Controller;
   6use App\Models\WbNotification;
   7use Illuminate\Http\Request;
   8
   9class NotificationController extends Controller
  10{
  11    public function index(Request $request)
  12    {
  13        $notifications = WbNotification::where('user_id', $request->user()->id)
  14            ->latest()
  15            ->paginate(20);
  16
  17        return response()->json($notifications);
  18    }
  19
  20    public function markRead(Request $request, $id)
  21    {
  22        WbNotification::where('id', $id)
  23            ->where('user_id', $request->user()->id)
  24            ->update(['is_read' => true]);
  25
  26        return response()->json(['message' => 'Marked as read.']);
  27    }
  28
  29    public function markAllRead(Request $request)
  30    {
  31        WbNotification::where('user_id', $request->user()->id)
  32            ->where('is_read', false)
  33            ->update(['is_read' => true]);
  34
  35        return response()->json(['message' => 'All notifications marked as read.']);
  36    }
  37
  38    public function unreadCount(Request $request)
  39    {
  40        $count = WbNotification::where('user_id', $request->user()->id)
  41            ->where('is_read', false)
  42            ->count();
  43
  44        return response()->json(['unread' => $count]);
  45    }
  46}
  47
backend/app/Http/Controllers/Api/SeekerController.php · 215 lines
   1<?php
   2
   3namespace App\Http\Controllers\Api;
   4
   5use App\Http\Controllers\Controller;
   6use App\Models\Resume;
   7use App\Models\SavedJob;
   8use App\Models\Seeker;
   9use App\Models\SeekerSkill;
  10use Illuminate\Http\Request;
  11use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Storage;
  12use Illuminate\Support\Str;
  13
  14class SeekerController extends Controller
  15{
  16    public function profile(Request $request)
  17    {
  18        $seeker = $request->user()->seeker()->with('skills', 'resumes')->first();
  19        return response()->json($seeker);
  20    }
  21
  22    public function updateProfile(Request $request)
  23    {
  24        $user = $request->user();
  25        $seeker = $user->seeker;
  26
  27        if (! $seeker) {
  28            $seeker = Seeker::create(['user_id' => $user->id]);
  29        }
  30
  31        $data = $request->validate([
  32            'headline' => 'nullable|string|max:255',
  33            'bio'      => 'nullable|string|max:2000',
  34            'location' => 'nullable|string|max:255',
  35            'phone'    => 'nullable|string|max:20',
  36            'skills'   => 'nullable|array',
  37            'skills.*' => 'string|max:100',
  38        ]);
  39
  40        $seeker->update([
  41            'headline' => $data['headline'] ?? $seeker->headline,
  42            'bio'      => $data['bio'] ?? $seeker->bio,
  43            'location' => $data['location'] ?? $seeker->location,
  44            'phone'    => $data['phone'] ?? $seeker->phone,
  45        ]);
  46
  47        if (isset($data['skills'])) {
  48            $seeker->skills()->delete();
  49            foreach ($data['skills'] as $skill) {
  50                SeekerSkill::create(['seeker_id' => $seeker->id, 'skill' => $skill]);
  51            }
  52        }
  53
  54        $strength = $this->calcStrength($seeker->fresh(['skills']));
  55        $seeker->update(['profile_strength' => $strength]);
  56
  57        return response()->json($seeker->fresh(['skills', 'resumes']));
  58    }
  59
  60    public function uploadPhoto(Request $request)
  61    {
  62        // 2MB cap (Laravel `max:` is in KB) on common image types.
  63        $request->validate(['photo' => 'required|image|max:2048']);
  64
  65        $seeker = $request->user()->seeker;
  66        if (! $seeker) {
  67            return response()->json(['message' => 'Seeker profile not found.'], 404);
  68        }
  69
  70        // Delete the previous photo if one exists — avoids orphaned files in storage.
  71        if ($seeker->profile_photo) {
  72            Storage::disk('public')->delete($seeker->profile_photo);
  73        }
  74
  75        $path = $request->file('photo')->store('avatars', 'public');
  76        $seeker->update(['profile_photo' => $path]);
  77
  78        return response()->json([
  79            'profile_photo'     => $path,
  80            'profile_photo_url' => Storage::disk('public')->url($path),
  81        ]);
  82    }
  83
  84    public function uploadResume(Request $request)
  85    {
  86        $request->validate(['resume' => 'required|file|mimes:pdf,doc,docx|max:5120']);
  87        $user   = $request->user();
  88        $seeker = $user->seeker;
  89
  90        if (! $seeker) {
  91            return response()->json(['message' => 'No seeker profile.'], 403);
  92        }
  93
  94        $file = $request->file('resume');
  95
  96        // Store in private disk (storage/app/resumes) — NOT web-accessible
  97        $storedName = Str::uuid() . '.' . $file->getClientOriginalExtension();
  98        $path       = $file->storeAs('resumes', $storedName, 'local');
  99
 100        $resume = Resume::create([
 101            'seeker_id'     => $seeker->id,
 102            'file_path'     => $path,
 103            'original_name' => $file->getClientOriginalName(),
 104            'is_primary'    => $seeker->resumes()->count() === 0,
 105        ]);
 106
 107        return response()->json($resume, 201);
 108    }
 109
 110    public function downloadResume(Request $request, $resumeId)
 111    {
 112        $user   = $request->user();
 113        $resume = Resume::findOrFail($resumeId);
 114        $seeker = $resume->seeker;
 115
 116        // Allow: the owning seeker, employers who have an application for this seeker, or admin
 117        $isOwner    = $seeker?->user_id === $user->id;
 118        $isEmployer = $user->role === 'employer' && $seeker?->applications()
 119            ->whereHas('jobListing', fn ($q) => $q->where('company_id', $user->company?->id))
 120            ->exists();
 121        $isAdmin    = $user->role === 'admin';
 122
 123        if (! $isOwner && ! $isEmployer && ! $isAdmin) {
 124            return response()->json(['message' => 'Forbidden.'], 403);
 125        }
 126
 127        if (! Storage::disk('local')->exists($resume->file_path)) {
 128            return response()->json(['message' => 'File not found.'], 404);
 129        }
 130
 131        return Storage::disk('local')->download(
 132            $resume->file_path,
 133            $resume->original_name
 134        );
 135    }
 136
 137    public function deleteResume(Request $request, $resumeId)
 138    {
 139        $seeker = $request->user()->seeker;
 140        $resume = Resume::findOrFail($resumeId);
 141
 142        if ($resume->seeker_id !== $seeker?->id) {
 143            return response()->json(['message' => 'Forbidden.'], 403);
 144        }
 145
 146        Storage::disk('local')->delete($resume->file_path);
 147        $resume->delete();
 148
 149        return response()->json(['message' => 'Resume deleted.']);
 150    }
 151
 152    public function savedJobs(Request $request)
 153    {
 154        $seeker = $request->user()->seeker;
 155
 156        if (! $seeker) {
 157            return response()->json(['data' => []]);
 158        }
 159
 160        // Order by saved_at — the saved_jobs table doesn't have created_at,
 161        // so the default `->latest()` (which assumes created_at) errors with
 162        // "Unknown column 'created_at'" on MySQL.
 163        $saved = SavedJob::with(['jobListing.company', 'jobListing.tags'])
 164            ->where('seeker_id', $seeker->id)
 165            ->orderByDesc('saved_at')
 166            ->paginate(20);
 167
 168        return response()->json($saved);
 169    }
 170
 171    public function saveJob(Request $request, $jobId)
 172    {
 173        $seeker = $request->user()->seeker;
 174
 175        if (! $seeker) {
 176            return response()->json(['message' => 'No seeker profile.'], 403);
 177        }
 178
 179        $existing = SavedJob::where('seeker_id', $seeker->id)
 180            ->where('job_listing_id', $jobId)
 181            ->first();
 182
 183        if ($existing) {
 184            return response()->json(['message' => 'Already saved.'], 422);
 185        }
 186
 187        SavedJob::create(['seeker_id' => $seeker->id, 'job_listing_id' => $jobId]);
 188        return response()->json(['message' => 'Job saved.'], 201);
 189    }
 190
 191    public function unsaveJob(Request $request, $jobId)
 192    {
 193        $seeker = $request->user()->seeker;
 194
 195        SavedJob::where('seeker_id', $seeker?->id)
 196            ->where('job_listing_id', $jobId)
 197            ->delete();
 198
 199        return response()->json(['message' => 'Job removed from saved.']);
 200    }
 201
 202    private function calcStrength(Seeker $seeker): int
 203    {
 204        $score = 0;
 205        if ($seeker->headline)              $score += 20;
 206        if ($seeker->bio)                   $score += 20;
 207        if ($seeker->location)              $score += 10;
 208        if ($seeker->phone)                 $score += 10;
 209        if ($seeker->profile_photo)         $score += 10;
 210        if ($seeker->skills->count() > 0)   $score += 15;
 211        if ($seeker->resumes()->count() > 0) $score += 15;
 212        return min($score, 100);
 213    }
 214}
 215
backend/app/Http/Controllers/Controller.php · 9 lines

   2
   3namespace App\Http\Controllers;
   4
   5abstract class Controller
   6{
   7    //
   8}

backend/app/Http/Middleware/CheckRole.php · 22 lines
   1<?php
   2
   3namespace App\Http\Middleware;
   4
   5use Closure;
   6use Illuminate\Http\Request;
   7use Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\Response;
   8
   9class CheckRole
  10{
  11    public function handle(Request $request, Closure $next, string ...$roles): Response
  12    {
  13        $user = $request->user();
  14
  15        if (! $user || ! in_array($user->role, $roles)) {
  16            return response()->json(['message' => 'Forbidden.'], 403);
  17        }
  18
  19        return $next($request);
  20    }
  21}
  22
backend/app/Models/Application.php · 34 lines
   1<?php
   2
   3namespace App\Models;
   4
   5use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model;
   6
   7class Application extends Model
   8{
   9    protected $fillable = [
  10        'job_listing_id', 'seeker_id', 'resume_id', 'cover_letter',
  11        'status', 'skill_fit', 'notes',
  12    ];
  13
  14    public function jobListing()
  15    {
  16        return $this->belongsTo(JobListing::class);
  17    }
  18
  19    public function seeker()
  20    {
  21        return $this->belongsTo(Seeker::class);
  22    }
  23
  24    public function resume()
  25    {
  26        return $this->belongsTo(Resume::class);
  27    }
  28
  29    public function timelines()
  30    {
  31        return $this->hasMany(ApplicationTimeline::class);
  32    }
  33}

backend/app/Models/Company.php · 42 lines
   1<?php
   2
   3namespace App\Models;
   4
   5use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model;
   6
   7class Company extends Model
   8{
   9    protected $fillable = [
  10        'user_id', 'name', 'slug', 'industry', 'size', 'founded_year',
  11        'website', 'description', 'address', 'city', 'province',
  12        'logo', 'cover_photo', 'verified_status', 'verified_at',
  13    ];
  14
  15    protected function casts(): array
  16    {
  17        return [
  18            'verified_at' => 'datetime',
  19        ];
  20    }
  21
  22    public function user()
  23    {
  24        return $this->belongsTo(User::class);
  25    }
  26
  27    public function jobListings()
  28    {
  29        return $this->hasMany(JobListing::class);
  30    }
  31
  32    public function verificationDocuments()
  33    {
  34        return $this->hasMany(VerificationDocument::class);
  35    }
  36
  37    public function reports()
  38    {
  39        return $this->hasMany(Report::class, 'target_id')->where('type', 'company');
  40    }
  41}

backend/app/Models/JobListing.php · 49 lines
   1<?php
   2
   3namespace App\Models;
   4
   5use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model;
   6
   7class JobListing extends Model
   8{
   9    protected $fillable = [
  10        'company_id', 'category_id', 'title', 'description', 'requirements',
  11        'location', 'type', 'experience_level', 'salary_min', 'salary_max',
  12        'slots', 'deadline', 'status',
  13    ];
  14
  15    protected function casts(): array
  16    {
  17        return [
  18            'deadline' => 'date',
  19            'salary_min' => 'decimal:2',
  20            'salary_max' => 'decimal:2',
  21        ];
  22    }
  23
  24    public function company()
  25    {
  26        return $this->belongsTo(Company::class);
  27    }
  28
  29    public function category()
  30    {
  31        return $this->belongsTo(Category::class);
  32    }
  33
  34    public function tags()
  35    {
  36        return $this->hasMany(JobTag::class);
  37    }
  38
  39    public function applications()
  40    {
  41        return $this->hasMany(Application::class);
  42    }
  43
  44    public function savedByUsers()
  45    {
  46        return $this->hasMany(SavedJob::class);
  47    }
  48}

backend/app/Models/SavedJob.php · 34 lines
   1<?php
   2
   3namespace App\Models;
   4
   5use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model;
   6
   7class SavedJob extends Model
   8{
   9    /**
  10     * The saved_jobs table uses a composite primary key (seeker_id + job_listing_id)
  11     * and a custom `saved_at` column instead of Laravel's default created_at/updated_at.
  12     * Without these flags Eloquent tries to insert created_at/updated_at columns
  13     * that don't exist, causing a 500 on every save.
  14     */
  15    public $timestamps  = false;
  16    public $incrementing = false;
  17
  18    protected $fillable = ['seeker_id', 'job_listing_id'];
  19
  20    protected $casts = [
  21        'saved_at' => 'datetime',
  22    ];
  23
  24    public function seeker()
  25    {
  26        return $this->belongsTo(Seeker::class);
  27    }
  28
  29    public function jobListing()
  30    {
  31        return $this->belongsTo(JobListing::class);
  32    }
  33}
  34

   2
   3namespace App\Models;
   4
   5use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model;
   6
   7class Seeker extends Model
   8{
   9    protected $fillable = [
  10        'user_id', 'headline', 'bio', 'location', 'phone',
  11        'profile_photo', 'profile_strength',
  12    ];
  13
  14    public function user()
  15    {
  16        return $this->belongsTo(User::class);
  17    }
  18
  19    public function skills()
  20    {
  21        return $this->hasMany(SeekerSkill::class);
  22    }
  23
  24    public function resumes()
  25    {
  26        return $this->hasMany(Resume::class);
  27    }
  28
  29    public function applications()
  30    {
  31        return $this->hasMany(Application::class);
  32    }
  33
  34    public function savedJobs()
  35    {
  36        return $this->hasMany(SavedJob::class);
  37    }
  38}

backend/app/Models/User.php · 70 lines
   1<?php
   2
   3namespace App\Models;
   4
   5use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Factories\HasFactory;
   6use Illuminate\Foundation\Auth\User as Authenticatable;
   7use Illuminate\Notifications\Notifiable;
   8use Laravel\Sanctum\HasApiTokens;
   9
  10class User extends Authenticatable
  11{
  12    use HasApiTokens, HasFactory, Notifiable;
  13
  14    protected $fillable = [
  15        'name',
  16        'email',
  17        'password',
  18        'role',
  19        'status',
  20    ];
  21
  22    protected $hidden = [
  23        'password',
  24        'remember_token',
  25    ];
  26
  27    protected function casts(): array
  28    {
  29        return [
  30            'email_verified_at' => 'datetime',
  31            'password' => 'hashed',
  32        ];
  33    }
  34
  35    public function seeker()
  36    {
  37        return $this->hasOne(Seeker::class);
  38    }
  39
  40    public function company()
  41    {
  42        return $this->hasOne(Company::class);
  43    }
  44
  45    public function auditLogs()
  46    {
  47        return $this->hasMany(AuditLog::class, 'actor_id');
  48    }
  49
  50    public function sentMessages()
  51    {
  52        return $this->hasMany(Message::class, 'sender_id');
  53    }
  54
  55    public function receivedMessages()
  56    {
  57        return $this->hasMany(Message::class, 'recipient_id');
  58    }
  59
  60    public function notifications()
  61    {
  62        return $this->hasMany(WbNotification::class);
  63    }
  64
  65    public function reports()
  66    {
  67        return $this->hasMany(Report::class, 'reporter_id');
  68    }
  69}
  70
